message.content:
Explore the cuTile Python IR subsystem at /home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/nvidia-cutile-python/src/cuda/tile/_ir/ . I need a concise architectural summary (medium breadth).

Specifically read and summarize:
- src/cuda/tile/_ir/ir.py (core IR: IRContext, Builder, Block, Var, Value, Op base classes, TypingHooks)
- src/cuda/tile/_ir/hir.py (High-level IR: Function, the HIR representation)
- src/cuda/tile/_ir/ops.py and _ir/core_ops.py (operation definitions, tile_impl_registry)
- src/cuda/tile/_ir/arithmetic_ops.py, _ir/cast_ops.py, _ir/control_flow_ops.py (how ops are defined)
- src/cuda/tile/_ir/type.py (TileTy, ArrayTy, ListTy type system)
- src/cuda/tile/_ir/scope.py, _ir/op_impl.py, _ir/ops_utils.py

For each, report:
1. The main classes/functions defined and their role.
2. How operations are registered and built (the Builder pattern).
3. The relationship between HIR and IR (what each represents).
4. Key extension points (e.g. tile_impl_registry, TypingHooks, OpImpl).

Report the actual class names and the key method names with file:line references. Do NOT dump whole files — give me a structured summary I can use to write a teaching outline. Keep it under 600 words.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) <2025> NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
2	#
3	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
4	
5	
6	# HIR stands for "High-level Intermediate Representation".
7	# The HIR is the initial representation that we build from the Python AST (see ast2hir.py).
8	#
9	# Unlike the IR, it doesn't have specific Operation definitions. Instead, it uses the concept
10	# of a "function call" to model all operations, including structured control flow.
11	# For example, addition like `a + b` is represented as calling `operator.add(a, b)`.
12	#
13	# It also has a simpler representation of constants: they can be used directly as arguments
14	# or as functions to be called (see the `Operand` type alias below).
15	
16	
17	import enum
18	import inspect
19	import threading
20	from dataclasses import dataclass
21	from textwrap import indent
22	from typing import Any, Set, Callable
23	
24	from cuda.tile._exception import Loc, FunctionDesc
25	
26	
27	@dataclass(frozen=True)
28	class Value:
29	    id: int
30	
31	    def __str__(self):
32	        return f"%{self.id}"
33	
34	
35	# Cache Value objects for reuse
36	_value_cache = []
37	_value_cache_lock = threading.Lock()
38	
39	
40	def make_value(id: int) -> Value:
41	    try:
42	        # Fast path
43	        return _value_cache[id]
44	    except IndexError:
45	        pass
46	
47	    if id >= 2000:
48	        # Don't cache too many objects
49	        return Value(id)
50	
51	    # Add 100 objects at a time to avoid triggering the slow path every time
52	    with _value_cache_lock:
53	        _value_cache.extend([Value(j) for j in range(len(_value_cache), id + 100)])
54	    return _value_cache[id]
55	
56	
57	# An "Operand" is a value that can be used as a function's argument, or as the function itself.
58	# There are two kinds of Operands:
59	#    - Using a `Value` instance as an Operand signals that this Operand is a result
60	#      of a previous call, or a kernel parameter.
61	#    - An object of any other type means that it is an immediate constant.
62	Operand = Value | Any
63	
64	
65	ModuleType = type(enum)
66	
67	
68	@dataclass
69	class Starred:
70	    value: Value
71	
72	
73	@dataclass
74	class Call:
75	    result: Value | None
76	    callee: Operand
77	    args: tuple[Operand | Starred, ...]
78	    kwargs: tuple[tuple[str | None, Operand], ...]  # None means an **unpacked argument
79	    loc: Loc
80	
81	    def __str__(self):
82	        opfmt = _OperandFormatter([])
83	        loc_str = f"  # Line {self.loc.line}"
84	        from cuda.tile._ir.hir_stubs import identity
85	        if self.callee is identity:
86	            return f"{self.result} = {opfmt(self.args[0])}{loc_str}"
87	        lhs_str = "" if self.result is None else f"{self.result} = "
88	        callee_str = opfmt(self.callee)
89	        args_and_kwargs = (*(opfmt(a) for a in self.args),
90	                           *(f"{k}={opfmt(v)}" for k, v in self.kwargs))
91	        args_str = ", ".join(args_and_kwargs)
92	        blocks_str = "".join(indent(f"\n{b}", "    ") for b in opfmt.blocks)
93	        return f"{lhs_str}{callee_str}({args_str}){loc_str}{blocks_str}"
94	
95	
96	class Jump(enum.Enum):
97	    END_BRANCH = "end_branch"
98	    CONTINUE = "continue"
99	    BREAK = "break"
100	    RETURN = "return"
101	
102	
103	@dataclass
104	class Block:
105	    block_id: int
106	    params: tuple[Value, ...]
107	    calls: list[Call]
108	    have_result: bool
109	    result: Operand
110	    jump: Jump | None
111	    jump_loc: Loc
112	    stored_indices: Set[int]
113	    loc: Loc
114	
115	    def __str__(self):
116	        params_str = ", ".join(str(p) for p in self.params)
117	        calls_str = "".join(f"\n{c}" for c in self.calls)
118	        if self.jump is not None:
119	            calls_str += "\n" + self.jump_str()
120	        calls_str = indent(calls_str, "    ")
121	        return f"^{self.block_id}({params_str}):{calls_str}"
122	
123	    def jump_str(self):
124	        opfmt = _OperandFormatter([])
125	        results_str = "" if self.result is None else opfmt(self.result)
126	        return f"{self.jump._value_}{results_str}  # Line {self.jump_loc.line}"
127	
128	
129	# Resolved position of a named variable.
130	#
131	# If `depth` is -1, then this is a global variable, and the `index`
132	# points into `hir.Function.frozen_global_names` / `hir.Function.frozen_global_values`.
133	# If both `depth` and `index` are -1, then this is an indication that no variable
134	# with the given name has been found.
135	#
136	# If `depth` is non-negative but is less than the depth of the current function,
137	# then this is a captured local variable of an enclosing function.
138	# In this case, the local variable name can be found in
139	# `hir.Function.enclosing_funcs[depth].local_names[index].
140	#
141	# If `depth` is equal to the depth of the current function, then this is a local variable.
142	# In this case, the local variable name can be found in `hir.Function.local_names[index]`.
143	@dataclass(frozen=True)
144	class ResolvedName:
145	    depth: int
146	    index: int
147	
148	
149	UNKNOWN_NAME = ResolvedName(-1, -1)
150	
151	
152	@dataclass(eq=False, repr=False)
153	class Function:
154	    desc: FunctionDesc
155	    body: Block
156	
157	    # For nested functions, this signature is synthesized from AST. In this case,
158	    # default values of parameters are represented with ClosureDefaultPlaceholder objects.
159	    signature: inspect.Signature
160	
161	    # Names of all local variables defined in this function.
162	    local_names: tuple[str, ...]
163	
164	    # For each function parameter, an index into `local_names`
165	    param_local_indices: tuple[int, ...]
166	
167	    # For each function parameter, its location (same length as `param_local_indices`)
168	    param_locs: tuple[Loc, ...]
169	
170	    # Names of all global-like variables accessible by this function.
171	    frozen_global_names: tuple[str, ...]
172	
173	    # Values of all global-like variables access by this function.
174	    # We assume that they don't change after compilation, hence the name "frozen".
175	    # Same length as `frozen_global_names`.
176	    frozen_global_values: tuple[Any, ...]
177	
178	    # Strict upper bound on hir.Value.id inside this function definition,
179	    # excluding any nested functions. Can be used to pre-allocate lists etc.
180	    value_id_upper_bound: int
181	
182	    # List of all function definitions nested directly inside this function.
183	    nested_functions: "tuple[Function, ...]"
184	
185	    # Pre-computed view of captured locals: for each non-negative `depth` strictly less than this
186	    # function's depth, the list of all local variable indices captured by this function.
187	    # Empty when this is a top-level function.
188	    captures_by_depth: tuple[tuple[int, ...], ...]
189	
190	    # Sequence of enclosing function definitions, outermost first.
191	    # Empty when this is a top-level function.
192	    enclosing_funcs: "tuple[Function, ...]"
193	
194	    def __repr__(self):
195	        return f"<HIR for function {self.desc}>"
196	
197	
198	@dataclass
199	class _OperandFormatter:
200	    blocks: list["Block"]
201	
202	    def __call__(self, x: Operand | Starred) -> str:
203	        if isinstance(x, Starred):
204	            assert isinstance(x.value, Value)
205	            return f"*{x}"
206	        elif isinstance(x, Value):
207	            return str(x)
208	        elif isinstance(x, ModuleType):
209	            return str(f"<mod:{x.__name__}>")
210	        elif isinstance(x, Block):
211	            self.blocks.append(x)
212	            return f"^{x.block_id}"
213	        elif callable(x):
214	            return f"<fn:{x.__name__}>"
215	        else:
216	            return f"<{repr(x)}>"
217	
218	
219	# ==================================
220	# Special function stubs used in HIR
221	# ==================================
222	
223	
224	class StaticEvalKind(enum.Enum):
225	    STATIC_EVAL = "static_eval()"
226	    STATIC_ASSERT_CONDITION = "static_assert() condition"
227	    STATIC_ASSERT_MESSAGE = "static_assert() message"
228	    STATIC_ITER_ITERABLE = "static_iter() iterable"
229	
230	
231	@dataclass
232	class StaticEvalExpression:
233	    compiled_expr: Callable
234	    kind: StaticEvalKind
235

2	#

4	
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	
7	from cuda.tile import TileTypeError, TileValueError
8	from cuda.tile._datatype import DType, is_pointer_dtype, PointerInfo, opaque_pointer_dtype, \
9	    pointer_dtype, is_numeric, numeric_dtype_category, NumericDTypeCategory, IntegerInfo, \
10	    _DTypePromotionImpl
11	from cuda.tile._ir.arithmetic_ops import astype
12	from cuda.tile._ir.ir import Operation, Var, operand, add_operation
13	from cuda.tile._ir.type import TensorLikeTy, LooselyTypedScalar
14	from cuda.tile._ir.typing_support import dtype_of_constant_scalar
15	from cuda.tile._memory_model import MemorySpace
16	
17	
18	@dataclass(eq=False)
19	class ReinterpretPointer(Operation, opcode="reinterpret_pointer"):
20	    pointer: Var[TensorLikeTy] = operand()
21	
22	
23	def reinterpret_pointer(pointer: Var[TensorLikeTy], target_ptr_dtype: DType) -> Var:
24	    src_ty = pointer.get_type()
25	    src_dtype = src_ty.tensor_dtype()
26	    assert is_pointer_dtype(src_dtype)
27	    assert is_pointer_dtype(target_ptr_dtype)
28	
29	    if src_dtype == target_ptr_dtype:
30	        return pointer
31	
32	    src_space = PointerInfo(src_dtype).memory_space
33	    target_space = PointerInfo(target_ptr_dtype).memory_space
34	    if src_space != target_space:
35	        raise TileTypeError(f"Source memory space '{src_space._name_}'"
36	                            f" does not match target memory space '{target_space}'")
37	
38	    return add_operation(ReinterpretPointer,
39	                         pointer.ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(
40	                                target_ptr_dtype, src_ty.tensor_shape()),
41	                         pointer=pointer)
42	
43	
44	@dataclass(eq=False)
45	class AddrSpaceCast(Operation, opcode="address_space_cast"):
46	    pointer: Var = operand()
47	
48	
49	def address_space_cast(value: Var[TensorLikeTy], memory_space: MemorySpace) -> Var:
50	    src_ty = value.get_type()
51	    src_dtype = src_ty.tensor_dtype()
52	    assert is_pointer_dtype(src_dtype)
53	
54	    info = PointerInfo(src_dtype)
55	    if info.memory_space == memory_space:
56	        return value
57	
58	    if info.opaque:
59	        new_dtype = opaque_pointer_dtype(memory_space)
60	    else:
61	        new_dtype = pointer_dtype(info.pointee_dtype, memory_space)
62	    result_ty = value.ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(new_dtype, src_ty.tensor_shape())
63	    return add_operation(AddrSpaceCast, result_ty, pointer=value)
64	
65	
66	def check_implicit_cast(src_ty: TensorLikeTy | LooselyTypedScalar, target_dtype: DType):
67	    if isinstance(src_ty, LooselyTypedScalar):
68	        if not is_numeric(target_dtype):
69	            raise TileValueError(f"cannot implicitly cast {src_ty.value}"
70	                                 f" to a non-numeric dtype {target_dtype}")
71	
72	        concrete_dtype = dtype_of_constant_scalar(src_ty.value)
73	        src_cat = numeric_dtype_category(concrete_dtype)
74	        dst_cat = numeric_dtype_category(target_dtype)
75	        if dst_cat == NumericDTypeCategory.Boolean:
76	            if src_cat not in (NumericDTypeCategory.Boolean, NumericDTypeCategory.Integral) \
77	                    or src_ty.value not in (0, 1):
78	                raise TileTypeError(f"cannot implicitly cast {src_ty.value} to {target_dtype}")
79	        elif src_cat > dst_cat:
80	            raise TileTypeError(f"cannot implicitly cast {src_ty.value} to {target_dtype}")
81	        elif src_cat == dst_cat == NumericDTypeCategory.Integral:
82	            info = IntegerInfo(target_dtype)
83	            if not (info.min <= src_ty.value <= info.max):
84	                raise TileValueError(f"{src_ty.value} is out of range of {target_dtype}")
85	    else:
86	        assert isinstance(src_ty, TensorLikeTy)
87	        if not _is_implicit_cast_ok(src_ty.tensor_dtype(), target_dtype):
88	            raise TileTypeError(f"cannot implicitly cast {src_ty.tensor_dtype()} to {target_dtype}")
89	
90	
91	def implicit_cast(src: Var[TensorLikeTy], target_dtype: DType, error_context: str) -> Var:
92	    ty = src.get_loose_type()
93	    try:
94	        check_implicit_cast(ty, target_dtype)
95	    except TileTypeError as e:
96	        raise TileTypeError(f"{error_context}: {str(e)}")
97	    except TileValueError as e:
98	        raise TileValueError(f"{error_context}: {str(e)}")
99	
100	    if is_pointer_dtype(target_dtype):
101	        p = address_space_cast(src, PointerInfo(target_dtype).memory_space)
102	        return reinterpret_pointer(p, target_dtype)
103	
104	    return astype(src, target_dtype)
105	
106	
107	def _is_implicit_cast_ok(src_dtype: DType, target_dtype: DType) -> bool:
108	    if is_numeric(src_dtype) and is_numeric(target_dtype):
109	        try:
110	            common_dtype = _DTypePromotionImpl.promote_dtypes(src_dtype, target_dtype)
111	        except TileTypeError:
112	            return False
113	        return common_dtype == target_dtype
114	    elif is_pointer_dtype(src_dtype) and is_pointer_dtype(target_dtype):
115	        src_info = PointerInfo(src_dtype)
116	        target_info = PointerInfo(target_dtype)
117	        if (src_info.memory_space != target_info.memory_space
118	                and target_info.memory_space != MemorySpace.GENERIC):
119	            # Only allow implicit cast to the GENERIC memory space
120	            return False
121	
122	        if src_info.opaque:
123	            # Disallow implicit opaque to concrete cast
124	            if not target_info.opaque:
125	                return False
126	        elif not target_info.opaque and src_info.pointee_dtype != target_info.pointee_dtype:
127	            # Disallow changing the pointee_dtype
128	            return False
129	
130	        return True
131	    else:
132	        return False
133

5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from typing import Sequence
7	
8	from typing_extensions import override
9	
10	from cuda.tile._datatype import TileTypeError, int64, is_pointer_dtype
11	from cuda.tile._ir import hir_stubs, hir
12	from cuda.tile._ir.aggregate_support import flatten_block_parameters, expand_aggregate_var, \
13	    flatten_aggregate_types, flatten_aggregates, unflatten_aggregates
14	from cuda.tile._ir.core_ops import store_var, store_invalid
15	from cuda.tile._ir.ir import Operation, Var, operand, Block, nested_block, Builder, LoopVarState, \
16	    PhiState, ConstantState, enter_nested_block, add_operation_variadic, add_operation
17	from cuda.tile._ir.op_impl import ImplRegistry, require_optional_range_type, \
18	    require_bool_scalar_type
19	from cuda.tile._ir.scope import Scope, ControlFlowInfo, JumpInfo
20	from cuda.tile._ir.type import InvalidType, TupleValue, RangeValue, NONE, TokenTy, Type
21	from cuda.tile._ir2bytecode import BytecodeContext, typeid, generate_bytecode_for_block
22	import cuda.tile._bytecode as bc
23	
24	
25	_registry = ImplRegistry()
26	impl = _registry.impl
27	
28	
29	def control_flow_impl_registry() -> ImplRegistry:
30	    return _registry
31	
32	
33	@dataclass(eq=False)
34	class Loop(Operation, opcode="loop"):
35	    start: Var | None = operand()
36	    stop: Var | None = operand()
37	    step: Var | None = operand()
38	    initial_values: tuple[Var, ...] = operand()
39	    body: Block = nested_block()
40	
41	    @property
42	    def is_for_loop(self) -> bool:
43	        return self.start is not None
44	
45	    @property
46	    def induction_var(self):
47	        assert self.is_for_loop
48	        return self.nested_blocks[0].params[0]
49	
50	    @property
51	    def body_vars(self) -> tuple[Var, ...]:
52	        return self.body.params[1:] if self.is_for_loop else self.body.params
53	
54	    @override
55	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[bc.Value, ...]:
56	        types = tuple(x.get_type() for x in self.body_vars)
57	        initial_values = [ctx.get_value(input_var)
58	                          for input_var in self.initial_values]
59	        result_type_ids = [typeid(ctx.type_table, ty) for ty in types]
60	
61	        if self.is_for_loop:
62	            start, stop, step = (ctx.get_value(x) for x in (self.start, self.stop, self.step))
63	            nested_builder = bc.encode_ForOp(ctx.builder, result_type_ids, start, stop, step,
64	                                             initial_values, unsignedCmp=False)
65	            induction_var_type_id = ctx.typeid_of(self.induction_var)
66	            block_arg_type_ids = (induction_var_type_id, *result_type_ids)
67	        else:
68	            nested_builder = bc.encode_LoopOp(ctx.builder, result_type_ids, initial_values)
69	            block_arg_type_ids = result_type_ids
70	
71	        with nested_builder.new_block(block_arg_type_ids) as block_args, ctx.enter_loop(self):
72	            block_args = iter(block_args)
73	            if self.is_for_loop:
74	                ctx.set_value(self.induction_var, next(block_args))
75	            for var, value in zip(self.body_vars, block_args, strict=True):
76	                ctx.set_value(var, value)
77	            generate_bytecode_for_block(ctx, self.body)
78	
79	        return nested_builder.done()
80	
81	    @override
82	    def _to_string_block_prefixes(self) -> list[str]:
83	        return ["do"]
84	
85	    @override
86	    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
87	        def format_var(var):
88	            ty = var.try_get_type()
89	            if ty is None:
90	                return var.name
91	            return f"{var.name}: {ty}"
92	
93	        if self.is_for_loop:
94	            body_vars = self.body.params[1:]
95	            header_str = (f"for {self.body.params[0].name}"
96	                          f" in range({self.start.name}, {self.stop.name}, {self.step.name})")
97	        else:
98	            body_vars = self.body.params
99	            header_str = "loop"
100	
101	        carried_vars_str = ", ".join(f"{format_var(b)} = {i.name}"
102	                                     for b, i in zip(body_vars, self.initial_values))
103	        return f"{header_str} (with {carried_vars_str})"

106	@impl(hir_stubs.loop)
107	async def loop_impl(body: hir.Block, iterable: Var):
108	    from .._passes.hir2ir import dispatch_hir_block, retarget_loc
109	
110	    scope = Scope.get_current()
111	    range_ty = require_optional_range_type(iterable)
112	    if range_ty is None and body.jump == hir.Jump.BREAK and not _have_nested_jump(body.calls):
113	        # In ast2hir, we create a loop around the function body in order to support early returns.
114	        # But if there is no early return, we can remove the loop. In this case, the loop will only
115	        # have a "break" at the end of the loop body, and no other break/continue statements.
116	        info = ControlFlowInfo((), flatten=True)
117	        with scope.change_loop_info(info):
118	            await dispatch_hir_block(body)
119	        return
120	
121	    builder = Builder.get_current()
122	    stored_locals = tuple(sorted(body.stored_indices))
123	    var_states = tuple(LoopVarState(PhiState(initial_constant_state=ConstantState.NONCONSTANT),
124	                                    PhiState())
125	                       for _ in stored_locals)
126	    initial_values = tuple(scope.local.get(index, builder.loc) for index in stored_locals)
127	    body_params = []
128	
129	    # Logic specific to `for` loops:
130	    if range_ty is not None:
131	        # A `for` loop may have 0 iterations, so initial values need to be propagated
132	        # to the loop's results.
133	        for initial_var, state in zip(initial_values, var_states, strict=True):
134	            state.result_phi.propagate(initial_var)
135	        # Create an induction variable
136	        induction_var = builder.ir_ctx.make_temp(builder.loc)
137	        induction_var.set_type(builder.ir_ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(range_ty.dtype, ()))
138	        scope.hir2ir_varmap[body.params[0].id] = induction_var
139	        body_params.append(induction_var)
140	
141	    # Process the loop body
142	    loop_info = ControlFlowInfo(stored_locals)
143	    body_loc = retarget_loc(body.loc, scope)
144	    with enter_nested_block(body_loc) as new_body, scope.change_loop_info(loop_info), \
145	            scope.local.enter_branch():
146	        # Define body variables. Not all of them will eventually be kept,
147	        # so we don't set the block parameters yet.
148	        body_vars = []
149	        for local_idx, initial_var, state in zip(
150	                stored_locals, initial_values, var_states, strict=True):
151	            state.body_phi.propagate(initial_var, allow_loose_typing=False)
152	            body_var = scope.local.redefine(local_idx, state.body_phi.last_loc)
153	            body_var.set_type(state.body_phi.ty)
154	            body_vars.append(body_var)
155	
156	        flat_body_vars = flatten_block_parameters(body_vars)
157	
158	        # Dispatch the body (hir.Block) to populate the new_body (ir.Block) with Operations
159	        await dispatch_hir_block(body)
160	
161	    # Propagate type information from Continue/Break to body/result phis
162	    for jump_info in loop_info.jumps:
163	        is_continue = isinstance(jump_info.jump_op, Continue)
164	        assert is_continue or isinstance(jump_info.jump_op, Break)
165	        for output, state in zip(jump_info.outputs, var_states, strict=True):
166	            if is_continue:
167	                state.body_phi.propagate(output, fail_eagerly=True)
168	            if range_ty is not None or not is_continue:
169	                state.result_phi.propagate(output)
170	
171	    # Determine the final loop variable types and filter out invalid variables
172	    mask = []
173	    for i, (body_var, state) in enumerate(zip(body_vars, var_states, strict=True)):
174	        was_valid = not isinstance(body_var.get_type_allow_invalid(), InvalidType)
175	        state.finalize_loopvar_type(body_var)
176	        ty = body_var.get_type_allow_invalid()
177	        is_valid = not isinstance(ty, InvalidType)
178	        mask.append(is_valid)
179	        if not was_valid and is_valid and ty.is_aggregate():
180	            # The initial variable is invalid but the loop variable is preserved,
181	            # and the loop variable is aggregate. In this case, `flat_body_vars[i]`
182	            # will contain a single variable (previously of InvalidType,
183	            # and now of an aggregate type). Thus, we need to update it with
184	            # the according number of flattened undefined variables.
185	            assert len(flat_body_vars[i]) == 1
186	            undefined_items = expand_aggregate_var(body_var)
187	            flat_body_vars[i] = undefined_items
188	            # Create a fake aggregate value so that flatten_aggregates() doesn't fail
189	            # when we update the Continue/Break statements later.
190	            body_var.set_aggregate(TupleValue(undefined_items))
191	
192	    # Set the block's parameters
193	    all_flattened_body_vars = sum((flattened for flattened, is_valid
194	                                   in zip(flat_body_vars, mask, strict=True) if is_valid), ())
195	    body_params.extend(all_flattened_body_vars)
196	    new_body.params = tuple(body_params)
197	    valid_var_types = tuple(v.get_type() for v, is_valid in zip(body_vars, mask, strict=True)
198	                            if is_valid)
199	    flat_var_types = flatten_aggregate_types(valid_var_types)
200	
201	    # Update Continue/Break statements
202	    for jump_info in loop_info.jumps:
203	        values = tuple(out
204	                       for out, is_valid in zip(jump_info.outputs, mask, strict=True)
205	                       if is_valid)
206	        flat_values = flatten_aggregates(values, valid_var_types)
207	        # For undefined break/continue value, add a MakeDummy op as its producer
208	        flat_values = _add_dummy_op_to_invalid_vars(flat_values, flat_var_types)
209	        assert len(flat_values) == len(all_flattened_body_vars)
210	        jump_info.jump_op.values = flat_values
211	
212	    # Create the loop Operation
213	    valid_initial_values = tuple(v for v, is_valid
214	                                 in zip(initial_values, mask, strict=True)
215	                                 if is_valid)
216	    flat_initial_values = flatten_aggregates(valid_initial_values, valid_var_types)
217	    # For undefined initial value, add a MakeDummy op as its producer
218	    flat_initial_values = _add_dummy_op_to_invalid_vars(flat_initial_values, flat_var_types)
219	    assert len(flat_initial_values) == len(all_flattened_body_vars)
220	
221	    if range_ty is None:
222	        start = stop = step = None
223	    else:
224	        range_val = iterable.get_aggregate()
225	        assert isinstance(range_val, RangeValue)
226	        start, stop, step = range_val.start, range_val.stop, range_val.step
227	    flat_result_vars = add_operation_variadic(Loop, flat_var_types,
228	                                              start=start,
229	                                              stop=stop,
230	                                              step=step,
231	                                              initial_values=flat_initial_values,
232	                                              body=new_body)
233	
234	    result_types = tuple(s.result_phi.ty for s, is_valid in zip(var_states, mask, strict=True)
235	                         if is_valid)
236	    result_vars = unflatten_aggregates(flat_result_vars, valid_var_types, result_types)
237	
238	    # Finalize the scope & type information for valid result variables
239	    valid_var_states = tuple(s for s, is_valid in zip(var_states, mask, strict=True)
240	                             if is_valid)
241	    valid_stored_locals = tuple(local_idx
242	                                for local_idx, is_valid in zip(stored_locals, mask, strict=True)
243	                                if is_valid)
244	    for res, state, local_idx in zip(result_vars, valid_var_states, valid_stored_locals,
245	                                     strict=True):
246	        state.result_phi.finalize_constant_and_loose_type(res)
247	        store_var(local_idx, res, state.result_phi.last_loc)
248	
249	    # For any names that are stored within the loop body but have an invalid result type,
250	    # we update the scope to point to an undefined variable of this invalid type, so that
251	    # using that variable afterwards would result in a type error.
252	    for body_var, state, local_idx, is_valid in zip(body_vars, var_states, stored_locals, mask,
253	                                                    strict=True):
254	        if not is_valid:
255	            store_invalid(local_idx, body_var.get_type_allow_invalid(),
256	                          state.result_phi.last_loc)
257	
258	    # Do this check at the end because this may be an automatically inserted loop
259	    # around the helper function's body.
260	    if builder.block_restriction is not None:
261	        builder.block_restriction.validate_operation(Loop)
262	
263	
264	def _have_nested_jump(calls: Sequence[hir.Call]) -> bool:
265	    return any(
266	        block.jump != hir.Jump.END_BRANCH or _have_nested_jump(block.calls)
267	        for c in calls
268	        if c.callee is hir_stubs.if_else
269	        for block in c.args[1:]
270	    )

273	@dataclass(eq=False)
274	class IfElse(Operation, opcode="ifelse"):
275	    cond: Var = operand()
276	    then_block: Block = nested_block()
277	    else_block: Block = nested_block()
278	
279	    @override
280	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[bc.Value, ...]:
281	        cond_val = ctx.get_value(self.cond)
282	        result_types = tuple(ctx.typeof(v) for v in self.result_vars)
283	        result_type_ids = tuple(typeid(ctx.type_table, t) for t in result_types)
284	        nested_builder = bc.encode_IfOp(ctx.builder, result_type_ids, cond_val)
285	
286	        for block in (self.then_block, self.else_block):
287	            with nested_builder.new_block(()):
288	                generate_bytecode_for_block(ctx, block)
289	
290	        return nested_builder.done()
291	
292	    @override
293	    def _to_string_block_prefixes(self) -> list[str]:
294	        return ["then", "else"]
295	
296	    @override
297	    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
298	        return f"if(cond={self.cond})"
299

301	@impl(hir_stubs.if_else)
302	async def if_else_impl(cond: Var, then_block: hir.Block, else_block: hir.Block) -> Var | None:
303	    from .._passes.hir2ir import dispatch_hir_block, retarget_loc
304	
305	    require_bool_scalar_type(cond)
306	    if cond.is_constant():
307	        branch_taken = then_block if cond.get_constant() else else_block
308	        return await _flatten_branch(branch_taken)
309	
310	    builder = Builder.get_current()
311	    if builder.block_restriction is not None:
312	        builder.block_restriction.validate_operation(IfElse)
313	
314	    # Get the total number of results by adding the number of stored variables.
315	    # Note: we sort the stored variable indices to make the order deterministic.
316	    scope = Scope.get_current()
317	    stored_locals = tuple(sorted(then_block.stored_indices | else_block.stored_indices))
318	
319	    # Convert the "then" branch from HIR to IR
320	    info = ControlFlowInfo(stored_locals)
321	    then_loc = retarget_loc(then_block.loc, scope)
322	    with enter_nested_block(then_loc) as new_then_block, scope.change_if_else_info(info), \
323	            scope.local.enter_branch():
324	        await dispatch_hir_block(then_block)
325	
326	    # If "then" branch doesn't yield, transform our if-else into the following:
327	    #    if cond:
328	    #        <then_block>
329	    #    else:
330	    #        EndBranch
331	    #    <else_block>
332	    # This is to avoid the situation where none of the branches yield.
333	    else_loc = retarget_loc(else_block.loc, scope)
334	    if len(info.jumps) == 0:
335	        info = ControlFlowInfo(())
336	        with enter_nested_block(else_loc) as new_else_block, scope.change_if_else_info(info), \
337	                scope.local.enter_branch():
338	            end_branch(None)
339	        add_operation_variadic(IfElse, (),
340	                               cond=cond, then_block=new_then_block, else_block=new_else_block)
341	        return await _flatten_branch(else_block)
342	
343	    # Convert the "else" branch from HIR to IR
344	    with enter_nested_block(else_loc) as new_else_block, scope.change_if_else_info(info), \
345	            scope.local.enter_branch():
346	        await dispatch_hir_block(else_block)
347	
348	    # Do type/constant propagation
349	    num_results = len(info.jumps[0].outputs)
350	    result_phis = tuple(PhiState() for _ in range(num_results))
351	    for jump_info in info.jumps:
352	        for phi, v in zip(result_phis, jump_info.outputs, strict=True):
353	            phi.propagate(v)
354	
355	    # Determine which results have valid types
356	    mask = tuple(not isinstance(phi.ty, InvalidType) for phi in result_phis)
357	    valid_result_types = tuple(phi.ty for phi, is_valid in zip(result_phis, mask) if is_valid)
358	
359	    # Update the EndBranch operations by setting the outputs
360	    for jump_info in info.jumps:
361	        outputs = tuple(v for v, is_valid in zip(jump_info.outputs, mask, strict=True)
362	                        if is_valid)
363	        flat_outputs = flatten_aggregates(outputs, valid_result_types)
364	        jump_info.jump_op.outputs = flat_outputs
365	
366	    # Generate an IfElse op
367	    flat_types = flatten_aggregate_types(valid_result_types)
368	    flat_results = add_operation_variadic(IfElse, flat_types, cond=cond,
369	                                          then_block=new_then_block, else_block=new_else_block)
370	    valid_results = unflatten_aggregates(flat_results, valid_result_types, valid_result_types)
371	
372	    # Finalize the constant/loose type information
373	    valid_result_phis = tuple(phi for phi, is_valid in zip(result_phis, mask) if is_valid)
374	    for var, phi in zip(valid_results, valid_result_phis, strict=True):
375	        phi.finalize_constant_and_loose_type(var)
376	
377	    it = iter(valid_results)
378	    all_results = tuple(next(it) if is_valid else None for is_valid in mask)
379	    assert next(it, None) is None
380	
381	    # Get/create variables for the explicit result
382	    num_explicit_results = num_results - len(stored_locals)
383	    if num_explicit_results == 0:
384	        ret = None
385	    else:
386	        assert num_explicit_results == 1
387	        ret = all_results[0]
388	        if ret is None:
389	            assert isinstance(result_phis[0].ty, InvalidType)
390	            ret = builder.ir_ctx.make_temp(builder.loc)
391	            ret.set_type(result_phis[0].ty)
392	
393	    # Update the scope for stored named
394	    for res_var, phi, local_idx in zip(all_results[num_explicit_results:],
395	                                       result_phis[num_explicit_results:],
396	                                       stored_locals, strict=True):
397	        if res_var is None:
398	            store_invalid(local_idx, phi.ty, phi.last_loc)
399	        else:
400	            store_var(local_idx, res_var, phi.last_loc)
401	
402	    return ret
403

410	                            " if the condition can be evaluated at compile time,"

416	async def _flatten_branch(branch: hir.Block) -> Var | None:
417	    from .._passes.hir2ir import dispatch_hir_block
418	    info = ControlFlowInfo((), flatten=True)
419	    with Scope.get_current().change_if_else_info(info):
420	        await dispatch_hir_block(branch)
421	    if len(info.jumps) == 0:
422	        return None
423	    else:
424	        assert len(info.jumps) == 1
425	        jump = info.jumps[0]
426	        assert len(jump.outputs) in (0, 1)
427	        return None if len(jump.outputs) == 0 else jump.outputs[0]

430	# Maps to ContinueOp in TileIR
431	@dataclass(eq=False)
432	class Continue(Operation, opcode="continue", terminator=True):
433	    values: tuple[Var, ...] = operand()
434	
435	    @override
436	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[()]:
437	        next_values = [ctx.get_value(var) for var in self.values]
438	        bc.encode_ContinueOp(ctx.builder, next_values)
439	        return ()
440	
441	    @override
442	    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
443	        return f"continue {', '.join([x.name for x in self.values])}"
444	
445	
446	def continue_():
447	    scope = Scope.get_current()
448	    info = scope.loop_info
449	    assert info is not None
450	    assert not info.flatten
451	
452	    for ctx_state in scope.context_stack[scope.loop_context_stack_depth:]:
453	        ctx_state.exit_callback()
454	
455	    builder = Builder.get_current()
456	    builder.add_operation_variadic(Continue, (), dict(values=()))
457	    op = builder.ops[-1]
458	    next_values = tuple(scope.local.get(local_idx, builder.loc) for local_idx in info.stored_locals)
459	    info.jumps.append(JumpInfo(op, next_values))

512	def end_branch(output: Var | None):
513	    scope = Scope.get_current()
514	    info = scope.if_else_info
515	    outputs = () if output is None else (output,)
516	    if info.flatten:
517	        op = None
518	    else:
519	        builder = Builder.get_current()
520	        builder.add_operation_variadic(EndBranch, (), dict(outputs=()))
521	        op = builder.ops[-1]
522	        outputs += tuple(scope.local.get(local_idx, builder.loc)
523	                         for local_idx in info.stored_locals)
524	    info.jumps.append(JumpInfo(op, outputs))

546	@dataclass(eq=False)
547	class MakeDummy(Operation, opcode="make_dummy"):
548	    """Placeholder value inserted for undefined variable in loop.
549	
550	    The use case for undefined variables is to represent loop's
551	    initial_values or continue/break's next_values during type inference or
552	    post dead code elimination.
553	    """
554	
555	    @override
556	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> bc.Value:
557	        ty = ctx.typeof(self.result_var)
558	        if isinstance(ty, TokenTy):
559	            return bc.encode_MakeTokenOp(ctx.builder, ctx.type_table.Token)
560	        from cuda.tile._ir.type import TileTy
561	        if isinstance(ty, TileTy) and is_pointer_dtype(ty.dtype):
562	            int_ty = TileTy(dtype=int64, shape=ty.shape)
563	            const = ctx.constant(0, int_ty)
564	            return bc.encode_IntToPtrOp(ctx.builder, typeid(ctx.type_table, ty), const)
565	        return ctx.constant(0, ty)
566	
567	
568	def _add_dummy_op_to_invalid_vars(vars: Sequence[Var],
569	                                  actual_types: Sequence[Type]) -> tuple[Var, ...]:
570	    return tuple(add_operation(MakeDummy, actual)
571	                 if isinstance(v.get_type_allow_invalid(), InvalidType)
572	                 else v
573	                 for v, actual in zip(vars, actual_types, strict=True))

31	class LocalScope:
32	    def __init__(self, local_names: tuple[str, ...], ir_ctx: IRContext):
33	        self._local_names = local_names
34	        self._ir_ctx = ir_ctx
35	        self._map: list[Var | None] = [None] * len(local_names)
36	        self.frozen = False
37	        self._dead = False
38	
39	    @staticmethod
40	    def create_frozen(local_names: tuple[str, ...],
41	                      frozen_indices: tuple[int, ...],
42	                      frozen_vars: tuple[Var, ...],
43	                      ir_ctx: IRContext):
44	        ret = LocalScope(local_names, ir_ctx)
45	        for idx, var in zip(frozen_indices, frozen_vars, strict=True):
46	            ret._map[idx] = var
47	        ret.frozen = True
48	        return ret
49	
50	    def mark_dead(self):
51	        self._dead = True
52	
53	    def redefine(self, index: int, loc: Loc) -> Var:
54	        assert not self._dead
55	        assert not self.frozen
56	        assert index >= 0
57	        var = self._ir_ctx.make_var(self._local_names[index], loc)
58	        self._map[index] = var
59	        return var
60	
61	    def __getitem__(self, index: int) -> Var:
62	        assert not self._dead
63	        assert index >= 0
64	        var = self._map[index]
65	        if var is None:
66	            raise TileSyntaxError(f"Undefined variable {self._local_names[index]} used")
67	        return var
68	
69	    def __setitem__(self, index: int, var: Var):
70	        assert not self._dead
71	        assert index >= 0
72	        self._map[index] = var
73	
74	    def get(self, index: int, loc: Loc):
75	        assert not self._dead
76	        assert index >= 0
77	        var = self._map[index]
78	        if var is None:
79	            name = self._local_names[index]
80	            var = self._ir_ctx.make_var(name, loc)
81	            var.set_type(InvalidType(f"Use of potentially undefined variable `{name}`", loc=loc))
82	        return var
83	
84	    @contextmanager
85	    def enter_branch(self):
86	        assert not self._dead
87	        old = self._map
88	        self._map = list(old)
89	        try:
90	            yield
91	        finally:
92	            self._map = old
93	
94	
95	class _CurrentScope(threading.local):
96	    scope = None
97	
98	
99	_current_scope = _CurrentScope()
100	
101	
102	class _MissingItem(enum.IntEnum):
103	    INSTANCE = 0
104	
105	
106	V = TypeVar("V")
107	
108	
109	class IntMap(Generic[V]):
110	    def __init__(self):
111	        self._items = []
112	
113	    def __getitem__(self, idx: int):
114	        assert isinstance(idx, int)
115	        assert idx >= 0
116	        try:
117	            val = self._items[idx]
118	        except IndexError:
119	            raise KeyError()
120	        if val is _MissingItem:
121	            raise KeyError()
122	        return val
123	
124	    def __setitem__(self, idx, value):
125	        assert isinstance(idx, int)
126	        assert idx >= 0
127	        size = len(self._items)
128	        if idx < size:
129	            self._items[idx] = value
130	        else:
131	            if idx > size:
132	                self._items.extend((_MissingItem.INSTANCE,) * (idx - size))
133	            self._items.append(value)
134	
135	
136	@dataclass(eq=False)
137	class Scope:
138	    local_scopes: tuple[LocalScope, ...]
139	    loop_info: ControlFlowInfo | None
140	    if_else_info: ControlFlowInfo | None
141	    call_site: Loc | None
142	    hir2ir_varmap: IntMap[Var]
143	    func_hir: hir.Function
144	    # FunctionDesc that represents the concrete specialization of `func_hir`
145	    # representing this scope.
146	    concrete_func_desc: FunctionDesc
147	    context_stack: list[ContextManagerState] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)
148	    loop_context_stack_depth: int | None = None
149	
150	    @property
151	    def local(self) -> LocalScope:
152	        return self.local_scopes[-1]
153	
154	    @property
155	    def depth(self) -> int:
156	        return len(self.local_scopes) - 1
157	
158	    @contextmanager
159	    def make_current(self):
160	        old = _current_scope.scope
161	        _current_scope.scope = self
162	        try:
163	            yield
164	        finally:
165	            _current_scope.scope = old
166	
167	    @staticmethod
168	    def get_current() -> "Scope | None":
169	        return _current_scope.scope
170	
171	    @contextmanager
172	    def change_loop_info(self, new: ControlFlowInfo):
173	        old_stack_depth = self.loop_context_stack_depth
174	        old = self.loop_info
175	        self.loop_info = new
176	        self.loop_context_stack_depth = len(self.context_stack)
177	        try:
178	            yield
179	        finally:
180	            self.loop_info = old
181	            self.loop_context_stack_depth = old_stack_depth
182	
183	    @contextmanager
184	    def change_if_else_info(self, new: ControlFlowInfo):
185	        old = self.if_else_info
186	        try:
187	            self.if_else_info = new
188	            yield
189	        finally:
190	            self.if_else_info = old
191

35	             RoundingMode.RM: None, RoundingMode.RP: None}
36	_RD_TRUEDIV = {**_RD_BASIC, RoundingMode.FULL: None, RoundingMode.APPROX: None}
37	_RD_SQRT = {**_RD_BASIC, RoundingMode.APPROX: None}
38	_RD_TANH = {RoundingMode.FULL: None, RoundingMode.APPROX: BytecodeVersion.V_13_2}
39	_RD_EXP = {RoundingMode.FULL: None, RoundingMode.APPROX: BytecodeVersion.V_13_3}

41	BINOP_REGISTRY = {
42	    "add": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: x + y, _RD_BASIC, support_flush_to_zero=True),
43	    "sub": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: x - y, _RD_BASIC, support_flush_to_zero=True),
44	    "mul": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: x * y, _RD_BASIC, support_flush_to_zero=True),
45	    "floordiv": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: x // y),
46	    "cdiv": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: (x + y - 1) // y),
47	    "truediv": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: x / y, _RD_TRUEDIV, support_flush_to_zero=True),
48	    "mod": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: x % y),
49	    "pow": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: x ** y),
50	    "atan2": MathOpDef(math.atan2),
51	    "max": MathOpDef(max, support_flush_to_zero=True),
52	    "min": MathOpDef(min, support_flush_to_zero=True),
53	    "and_": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: x & y),
54	    "or_": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: x | y),
55	    "xor": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: x ^ y),
56	    "eq": MathOpDef(lambda x, y: x == y),

82	def _invert(x: int | bool, bool_action: Literal['raise'] | Literal['not']):
83	    if isinstance(x, bool):
84	        if bool_action == 'not':
85	            return not x
86	        else:
87	            assert bool_action == 'raise'
88	            raise TileTypeError(
89	                '`~` on boolean constant is not supported, please use ct.bitwise_not')
90	    return ~x

148	def check_rd_and_ftz(fn: str, rounding_mode: Optional[RoundingMode], flush_to_zero: bool,
149	                     dtype: datatype.DType):
150	    if rounding_mode is None and flush_to_zero is False:
151	        return
152	
153	    math_op_def = BINOP_REGISTRY[fn] if fn in BINOP_REGISTRY else UNARYOP_REGISTRY[fn]
154	    if rounding_mode is not None:
155	        if rounding_mode not in math_op_def.supported_rounding_modes:
156	            raise TileTypeError(
157	                f'Rounding mode {rounding_mode.value} is not supported for {fn}')
158	        min_version = math_op_def.supported_rounding_modes[rounding_mode]
159	        if min_version is not None:
160	            cur_version = Builder.get_current().ir_ctx.tileiras_version
161	            if cur_version < min_version:
162	                raise TileUnsupportedFeatureError(
163	                    f'{fn} rounding_mode={rounding_mode.value} requires tileiras '
164	                    f'{min_version.as_string()} or later. '
165	                    f'Current version is {cur_version.as_string()}.')
166	        if not datatype.is_unrestricted_float(dtype):
167	            raise TileTypeError(
168	                f'Rounding mode can only be used for unrestricted float types, '
169	                f'but got {dtype}')
170	        if rounding_mode in [RoundingMode.APPROX, RoundingMode.FULL]:
171	            if dtype != datatype.float32:
172	                raise TileTypeError(
173	                    f'Rounding mode {rounding_mode.value} can only be used for float32 type, '
174	                    f'but got {dtype}')
175	    if flush_to_zero:
176	        if not math_op_def.support_flush_to_zero:
177	            raise TileTypeError(f'Flush to zero is not supported for {fn}')
178	        if dtype != datatype.float32:
179	            raise TileTypeError(
180	                f'Flush to zero can only be used for float32 type, '
181	                f'but got {dtype}')

242	
243	def _promote_dtype_and_loosely_typed_constant(dtype: DType,
244	                                              loose_const: Any,
245	                                              force_float: bool) -> DType:
246	    loose_dtype = dtype_of_constant_scalar(loose_const)
247	
248	    cat = datatype.numeric_dtype_category(dtype)
249	    if cat == NumericDTypeCategory.RestrictedFloat:
250	        # Treat restricted floats as regular floats.
251	        cat = NumericDTypeCategory.Float
252	    loose_cat = datatype.numeric_dtype_category(loose_dtype)
253	
254	    if loose_cat == cat:
255	        # Both values are of the same dtype category. Use the concrete dtype in this case.
256	        ret = dtype
257	
258	        # For integers, verify that the loosely typed constant is within the range of dtype.
259	        if cat == NumericDTypeCategory.Integral and not force_float:
260	            info = datatype.IntegerInfo(dtype)
261	            if not (info.min <= loose_const <= info.max):
262	                raise TileValueError(f"Integer constant {loose_const} is out of range of {dtype}")
263	    else:
264	        # Strongest category always wins
265	        ret = loose_dtype if loose_cat > cat else dtype
266	
267	    return ret if not force_float or datatype.is_float(ret) else datatype.default_float_type
268	
269	
270	def promote_dtypes(t1: DType | LooselyTypedScalar,
271	                   t2: DType | LooselyTypedScalar,
272	                   force_float: bool = False) -> DType:
273	    match t1, t2:
274	        case LooselyTypedScalar(val1), LooselyTypedScalar(val2):
275	            dtype1 = dtype_of_constant_scalar(val1)
276	            dtype2 = dtype_of_constant_scalar(val2)
277	            return _DTypePromotionImpl.promote_dtypes(dtype1, dtype2, force_float)
278	        case LooselyTypedScalar(val), dtype:
279	            return _promote_dtype_and_loosely_typed_constant(dtype, val, force_float)
280	        case dtype, LooselyTypedScalar(val):
281	            return _promote_dtype_and_loosely_typed_constant(dtype, val, force_float)
282	        case dtype1, dtype2:
283	            return _DTypePromotionImpl.promote_dtypes(dtype1, dtype2, force_float)
284	
285	
286	def promote_types(t1: TensorLikeTy | LooselyTypedScalar,
287	                  t2: TensorLikeTy | LooselyTypedScalar,
288	                  typing_hooks: TypingHooks,
289	                  force_float: bool = False) -> TensorLikeTy:
290	    dtype_1 = t1 if isinstance(t1, LooselyTypedScalar) else t1.tensor_dtype()
291	    dtype_2 = t2 if isinstance(t2, LooselyTypedScalar) else t2.tensor_dtype()
292	    dtype = promote_dtypes(dtype_1, dtype_2, force_float)
293	    shape = broadcast_shapes2(t1.tensor_shape(), t2.tensor_shape())
294	    return typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(dtype, shape)
295	
296	
297	class BroadcastError(Exception):
298	    pass
299	
300	
301	# FIXME: rename to broadcast_shapes() after we remove broadcast_shapes()
302	def broadcast_shapes2(s1: Sequence[int], s2: Sequence[int]) -> Tuple[int, ...]:
303	    result_shape = []
304	    for d1, d2 in itertools.zip_longest(reversed(s1), reversed(s2), fillvalue=1):
305	        if d1 != d2 and d1 != 1 and d2 != 1:
306	            raise BroadcastError(f"Shapes are not broadcastable: {tuple(s1)}, {tuple(s2)}")
307	        result_shape.append(max(d1, d2))
308	    return tuple(reversed(result_shape))
309	
310	
311	def is_shape_broadcastable_to(src: Sequence[int], dst: Sequence[int]) -> bool:
312	    return len(src) <= len(dst) and all(x in (y, 1) for x, y in zip(reversed(src), reversed(dst)))
313	
314	
315	def get_default_order(rank: int) -> tuple[int, ...]:
316	    return tuple(range(rank))
317	
318	
319	def validate_memory_order_and_scope(
320	    memory_order: MemoryOrder,
321	    memory_scope: MemoryScope,
322	    operation_type: type[Operation],
323	):
324	    opcode: str = operation_type._opcode
325	    if memory_order not in operation_type.VALID_MEMORY_ORDERS:
326	        formatted_expected = ", ".join(
327	            str(order) for order in operation_type.VALID_MEMORY_ORDERS
328	        )
329	        raise TileTypeError(
330	            f"Invalid memory order for {opcode}. "
331	            f"Got {memory_order}, expected one of {formatted_expected}"
332	        )
333	
334	    if memory_scope not in operation_type.VALID_MEMORY_SCOPES:
335	        formatted_expected = ", ".join(
336	            str(scope) for scope in operation_type.VALID_MEMORY_SCOPES
337	        )
338	        raise TileTypeError(
339	            f"Invalid memory scope for {opcode}. "
340	            f"Got {memory_scope}, expected one of {formatted_expected}"
341	        )
342	
343	    if memory_order == MemoryOrder.WEAK and memory_scope != MemoryScope.NONE:
344	        raise TileTypeError(
345	            f"{opcode} with WEAK memory ordering cannot specify a memory scope"
346	        )
347	
348	    if memory_order != MemoryOrder.WEAK and memory_scope == MemoryScope.NONE:
349	        raise TileTypeError(
350	            f"{opcode} with {memory_order.name} memory ordering requires a memory scope"
351	        )

64	def reshape(x: Var[TensorLikeTy], new_shape: Sequence[int]) -> Var:
65	    x_ty = x.get_type()
66	    x_shape = x_ty.tensor_shape()
67	    numel = math.prod(x_shape)
68	
69	    negative_one_index = None
70	    numel2 = 1
71	    for i, dim_value in enumerate(new_shape):
72	        if dim_value < 0:
73	            if dim_value < -1:
74	                raise TileTypeError(f"Dimension can only be -1 or non-negative, got {dim_value}")
75	            if negative_one_index is not None:
76	                raise TileTypeError(f"Only one dimension can be -1, got {new_shape}")
77	            negative_one_index = i
78	        else:
79	            numel2 *= dim_value
80	
81	    if negative_one_index is not None:
82	        if numel2 == 0 or numel % numel2 != 0:
83	            raise TileTypeError(f"Cannot reshape {x_shape} to {new_shape}")
84	        new_shape = list(new_shape)
85	        new_shape[negative_one_index] = numel // numel2
86	        new_shape = tuple(new_shape)
87	    elif numel != numel2:
88	        raise TileTypeError(f"Cannot reshape {x_shape} to {new_shape}")
89	
90	    if new_shape == x_shape:
91	        return x
92	    else:
93	        res_type = x.ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(x_ty.tensor_dtype(), new_shape)
94	        return add_operation(TileReshape, res_type, x=x)
95

101	    @override
102	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> bc.Value:
103	        x_value = ctx.get_value(self.x)

108	def broadcast_to(x: Var[TensorLikeTy], shape: Sequence[int]) -> Var[TensorLikeTy]:
109	    x_ty = x.get_type()
110	    old_shape = x_ty.tensor_shape()
111	
112	    if not is_shape_broadcastable_to(old_shape, shape):
113	        raise TileTypeError(f"Shape {old_shape} is not broadcastable to {tuple(shape)}")
114	
115	    if len(shape) > len(old_shape):
116	        extra_ones = (1,) * (len(shape) - len(old_shape))
117	        old_shape = extra_ones + old_shape
118	        x = reshape(x, old_shape)
119	
120	    if old_shape == shape:
121	        return x
122	    else:
123	        result_ty = x.ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(x_ty.tensor_dtype(), shape)
124	        return add_operation(TileBroadcast, result_ty, x=x)

137	def astype(x: Var[TensorLikeTy], dtype: DType) -> Var[TensorLikeTy]:
138	    x_ty = x.get_type()
139	    if x_ty.tensor_dtype() == dtype:
140	        return x
141	
142	    if x.is_constant():
143	        val = numeric_dtype_category(dtype).pytype(x.get_constant())
144	        return strictly_typed_const(val, x.ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(dtype, ()))
145	
146	    result_ty = x.ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(dtype, x_ty.tensor_shape())
147	    return add_operation(TileAsType, result_ty, x=x)

150	def dtype_constructor(new_dtype: DType, x: Var) -> Var[TensorLikeTy]:
151	    if x.is_constant():
152	        pytype = numeric_dtype_category(new_dtype).pytype
153	        try:
154	            const_value = pytype(x.get_constant())
155	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
156	            raise TileTypeError(f"Invalid argument type for {new_dtype}")
157	        ty = x.ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(new_dtype, ())
158	        return strictly_typed_const(const_value, ty=ty)
159	
160	    x = ensure_scalar(x)
161	    return astype(x, new_dtype)

219	class RawBinaryBitwiseOperation(Operation, opcode="raw_binary_bitwise"):
220	    fn: str = attribute()

231	            case "or_": return bc.encode_OrIOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs)

245	def binary_bitwise_tensorlike(fn: str,
246	                              x: Var[TensorLikeTy],
247	                              y: Var[TensorLikeTy]) -> Var[TensorLikeTy]:
248	    x_ty = x.get_loose_type()
249	    y_ty = y.get_loose_type()
250	
251	    if isinstance(x_ty, LooselyTypedScalar) and isinstance(y_ty, LooselyTypedScalar):
252	        return binop_propagate_constant(fn, x_ty.value, y_ty.value, None)
253	
254	    lhs_dtype = x_ty.tensor_dtype()
255	    rhs_dtype = y_ty.tensor_dtype()
256	
257	    if not (is_integral(lhs_dtype) or is_boolean(lhs_dtype)) \
258	            or not (is_integral(rhs_dtype) or is_boolean(rhs_dtype)):
259	        raise TileTypeError("Bitwise operations require integers or booleans."
260	                            " Use an explicit cuda.tile.bitcast() for non-integer operands.")
261	
262	    x_loose = isinstance(x_ty, LooselyTypedScalar)
263	    y_loose = isinstance(y_ty, LooselyTypedScalar)
264	    if x_loose == y_loose and lhs_dtype != rhs_dtype:
265	        msg = "Bitwise operands must have same data type, got:"
266	        msg += f" {lhs_dtype} and {rhs_dtype}"
267	        raise TileTypeError(msg)
268	
269	    if {lhs_dtype, rhs_dtype} == {bool_, int8}:
270	        raise TileTypeError("Bitwise op does not support bool and int8")
271	
272	    common_ty = promote_types(x_ty, y_ty, x.ctx.typing_hooks)
273	    x = promote_and_broadcast_to(x, common_ty)
274	    y = promote_and_broadcast_to(y, common_ty)
275	
276	    if x.is_constant() and y.is_constant():
277	        return binop_propagate_constant(fn, x.get_constant(), y.get_constant(), common_ty)
278	
279	    return binary_bitwise_tensorlike_raw(fn, x, y)

356	# Does not do broadcasting or type promotion, hence the name "Raw"
357	@dataclass(eq=False)
358	class RawBinaryArithmeticOperation(Operation, opcode="raw_binary_arith"):
359	    fn: str = attribute()
360	    rounding_mode: RoundingMode | None = attribute()
361	    flush_to_zero: bool = attribute()
362	    lhs: Var[TensorLikeTy] = operand()
363	    rhs: Var[TensorLikeTy] = operand()
364	
365	    @override
366	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> bc.Value:
367	        result_ty = self.result_var.get_type()
368	        dtype = get_dtype(result_ty)
369	        kind = "float" if is_float(dtype) else "int"
370	        res_typeid = typeid(ctx.type_table, result_ty)
371	        rm = self.rounding_mode if self.rounding_mode is not None else get_default_rounding_mode()
372	        rounding_mode = rounding_mode_to_bytecode[rm]
373	        lhs = ctx.get_value(self.lhs)
374	        rhs = ctx.get_value(self.rhs)
375	
376	        match self.fn, kind:
377	            case "add", "int":
378	                return bc.encode_AddIOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
379	                                        overflow=bc.IntegerOverflow.NONE)
380	            case "add", "float":
381	                return bc.encode_AddFOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
382	                                        rounding_mode=rounding_mode,
383	                                        flush_to_zero=self.flush_to_zero)
384	            case "sub", "int":
385	                return bc.encode_SubIOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
386	                                        overflow=bc.IntegerOverflow.NONE)
387	            case "sub", "float":
388	                return bc.encode_SubFOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
389	                                        rounding_mode=rounding_mode,
390	                                        flush_to_zero=self.flush_to_zero)
391	            case "mul", "int":
392	                return bc.encode_MulIOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
393	                                        overflow=bc.IntegerOverflow.NONE)
394	            case "mul", "float":
395	                return bc.encode_MulFOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
396	                                        rounding_mode=rounding_mode,
397	                                        flush_to_zero=self.flush_to_zero)
398	            case "floordiv", "int":
399	                return bc.encode_DivIOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
400	                                        signedness=get_signedness(dtype),
401	                                        rounding=bc.RoundingMode.NEGATIVE_INF)
402	            case "floordiv", "float":
403	                quotient = bc.encode_DivFOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
404	                                            rounding_mode=rounding_mode,
405	                                            flush_to_zero=self.flush_to_zero)
406	                return bc.encode_FloorOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, quotient)
407	            case "cdiv", "int":
408	                return bc.encode_DivIOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
409	                                        signedness=get_signedness(dtype),
410	                                        rounding=bc.RoundingMode.POSITIVE_INF)
411	            case "truediv", "float":
412	                return bc.encode_DivFOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
413	                                        rounding_mode=rounding_mode,
414	                                        flush_to_zero=self.flush_to_zero)
415	            case "pow", "float":
416	                return bc.encode_FPowFOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs)
417	            case "atan2", "float":
418	                return bc.encode_Atan2Op(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs)
419	            case "min", "int":
420	                return bc.encode_MinIOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
421	                                        signedness=get_signedness(dtype))
422	            case "min", "float":
423	                return bc.encode_MinFOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
424	                                        propagate_nan=False,
425	                                        flush_to_zero=self.flush_to_zero)
426	            case "max", "int":
427	                return bc.encode_MaxIOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
428	                                        signedness=get_signedness(dtype))
429	            case "max", "float":
430	                return bc.encode_MaxFOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
431	                                        propagate_nan=False,
432	                                        flush_to_zero=self.flush_to_zero)
433	            case "c_mod", "float":
434	                # C-style modulo
435	                return bc.encode_RemFOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs)
436	            case "c_mod", "int":
437	                # C-style modulo
438	                return bc.encode_RemIOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, lhs, rhs,
439	                                        signedness=get_signedness(dtype))
440	            case _:
441	                raise NotImplementedError(f"Missing binary arithmetic implementation"
442	                                          f" for {self.fn}, {kind}")
443	
444	
445	def binary_arithmetic_tensorlike_raw(fn: str, x: Var[TensorLikeTy], y: Var[TensorLikeTy],
446	                                     rounding_mode: RoundingMode | None = None,
447	                                     flush_to_zero: bool = False) -> Var[TensorLikeTy]:
448	    ty = x.get_type()
449	    assert ty == y.get_type(), f"{ty} != {y.get_type()}"
450	    # FIXME: remove cutile-specific check
451	    check_rd_and_ftz(fn, rounding_mode, flush_to_zero, ty.tensor_dtype())
452	    return add_operation(RawBinaryArithmeticOperation, ty, fn=fn, lhs=x, rhs=y,
453	                         rounding_mode=rounding_mode, flush_to_zero=flush_to_zero)
454	
455	
456	def binary_arithmetic_tensorlike(fn: str, x: Var[TensorLikeTy], y: Var[TensorLikeTy],
457	                                 rounding_mode: RoundingMode | None = None,
458	                                 flush_to_zero: bool = False) -> Var[TensorLikeTy]:
459	    x_ty = x.get_loose_type()
460	    y_ty = y.get_loose_type()
461	
462	    if not is_arithmetic(x_ty.tensor_dtype()):
463	        raise TileTypeError(f"Left-hand side has non-arithmetic dtype {x_ty.tensor_dtype()}")
464	    if not is_arithmetic(y_ty.tensor_dtype()):
465	        raise TileTypeError(f"Right-hand side has non-arithmetic dtype {y_ty.tensor_dtype()}")
466	
467	    if isinstance(x_ty, LooselyTypedScalar) and isinstance(y_ty, LooselyTypedScalar):
468	        return binop_propagate_constant(fn, x_ty.value, y_ty.value, None)
469	
470	    force_float = (fn == "truediv")
471	    common_ty = promote_types(x_ty, y_ty, x.ctx.typing_hooks, force_float=force_float)
472	
473	    common_dtype = common_ty.tensor_dtype()
474	    if common_dtype == bool_:
475	        raise TileTypeError(f'Binary arithmetic op `{fn}` does not support bool, '
476	                            f'please cast bool to int')
477	
478	    x = promote_and_broadcast_to(x, common_ty)
479	    y = promote_and_broadcast_to(y, common_ty)
480	
481	    if x.is_constant() and y.is_constant():
482	        return binop_propagate_constant(fn, x.get_constant(), y.get_constant(), common_ty)
483	
484	    return binary_arithmetic_tensorlike_raw(fn, x, y, rounding_mode, flush_to_zero)
485

498	
499	def mod_tensorlike(x: Var[TensorLikeTy], y: Var[TensorLikeTy]) -> Var[TensorLikeTy]:
500	    x_ty = x.get_loose_type()
501	    y_ty = y.get_loose_type()
502	    if x_ty.tensor_dtype() == y_ty.tensor_dtype() == bool_:
503	        raise TileTypeError('Modulo operation does not support bool')
504	
505	    if isinstance(x_ty, LooselyTypedScalar) and isinstance(y_ty, LooselyTypedScalar):
506	        with reraise_tile_exception():
507	            res = x_ty.value % y_ty.value
508	        return loosely_typed_const(res)
509	
510	    # Usual promote & broadcast logic
511	    common_ty = promote_types(x_ty, y_ty, x.ctx.typing_hooks)
512	    x = promote_and_broadcast_to(x, common_ty)
513	    y = promote_and_broadcast_to(y, common_ty)
514	
515	    if x.is_constant() and y.is_constant():
516	        with reraise_tile_exception():
517	            res = x.get_constant() % y.get_constant()
518	        return strictly_typed_const(res, common_ty)
519	
520	    # TileOR rem follows the C behavior while Python's mod behavior differs.
521	    # So we generate the C-style mod first and then apply a correction.
522	    value = binary_arithmetic_tensorlike_raw("c_mod", x, y)
523	
524	    # If the sign of `value` does not match the sign of `y`, apply a correction.
525	    zero = strictly_typed_const(0, common_ty)
526	    value_sign = compare_tensorlike("lt", value, zero)
527	    y_sign = compare_tensorlike("lt", y, zero)
528	
529	    # need_fix = (value_sign ^ y_sign) & (value != 0)
530	    sign_mismatch = binary_bitwise_tensorlike("xor", value_sign, y_sign)
531	    value_not_zero = compare_tensorlike("ne", value, zero)
532	    need_fix = binary_bitwise_tensorlike("and_", sign_mismatch, value_not_zero)
533	
534	    fixed_value = binary_arithmetic_tensorlike("add", value, y)
535	    return where(need_fix, fixed_value, value)
536

543	# Does not support broadcasting or type promotion
544	@dataclass(eq=False)
545	class RawWhereOperation(Operation, opcode="raw_where"):
546	    cond: Var = operand()
547	    x: Var = operand()
548	    y: Var = operand()
549	
550	    @override
551	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> bc.Value:
552	        res_typeid = ctx.typeid_of(self.result_var)
553	        cond = ctx.get_value(self.cond)
554	        x = ctx.get_value(self.x)
555	        y = ctx.get_value(self.y)
556	        return bc.encode_SelectOp(ctx.builder, res_typeid, cond, x, y)
557	
558	
559	def where_raw(cond: Var[TensorLikeTy],
560	              x: Var[TensorLikeTy],
561	              y: Var[TensorLikeTy]) -> Var[TensorLikeTy]:
562	    ty = x.get_type()
563	    assert ty == y.get_type()
564	    return add_operation(RawWhereOperation, ty, cond=cond, x=x, y=y)
565	
566	
567	def where(cond: Var[TensorLikeTy],
568	          x: Var[TensorLikeTy],
569	          y: Var[TensorLikeTy]) -> Var[TensorLikeTy]:
570	    cond_ty = cond.get_loose_type()
571	    x_ty = x.get_loose_type()
572	    y_ty = y.get_loose_type()
573	
574	    typing_hooks = cond.ctx.typing_hooks
575	
576	    xy_ty = promote_types(x_ty, y_ty, typing_hooks)
577	    dtype = xy_ty.tensor_dtype()
578	
579	    cond_like_ty = typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(dtype, cond_ty.tensor_shape())
580	    res_ty = promote_types(cond_like_ty, xy_ty, typing_hooks)
581	
582	    result_shaped_bool = typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(bool_, res_ty.tensor_shape())
583	
584	    cond = promote_and_broadcast_to(cond, result_shaped_bool)
585	    x = promote_and_broadcast_to(x, res_ty)
586	    y = promote_and_broadcast_to(y, res_ty)
587	    return where_raw(cond, x, y)
588

590	@dataclass(eq=False)
591	class Unary(Operation, opcode="unaryop"):
592	    fn: str = attribute()
593	    rounding_mode: RoundingMode | None = attribute()
594	    flush_to_zero: bool = attribute()
595	    operand: Var = operand()
596	
597	    @override
598	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> bc.Value:
599	        x = ctx.get_value(self.operand)
600	        rm = (self.rounding_mode if self.rounding_mode is not None
601	              else get_default_rounding_mode(self.fn))
602	        rounding_mode = rounding_mode_to_bytecode[rm]
603	        flush_to_zero = self.flush_to_zero
604	        input_type = ctx.typeof(self.operand)
605	        input_dtype = get_dtype(input_type)
606	        flt = is_float(input_dtype)
607	        res_type_id = ctx.typeid_of(self.result_var)
608	
609	        match self.fn, flt:
610	            case "abs", True: return bc.encode_AbsFOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
611	            case "abs", False: return bc.encode_AbsIOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
612	            case "neg", True: return bc.encode_NegFOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
613	            case "neg", False: return bc.encode_NegIOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x,
614	                                                       bc.IntegerOverflow.NONE)
615	            case "exp", True: return bc.encode_ExpOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x,
616	                                                     rounding_mode=rounding_mode)
617	            case "exp2", True: return bc.encode_Exp2Op(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x,
618	                                                       flush_to_zero=flush_to_zero)
619	            case "sin", True: return bc.encode_SinOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
620	            case "cos", True: return bc.encode_CosOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
621	            case "sinh", True: return bc.encode_SinHOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
622	            case "cosh", True: return bc.encode_CosHOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
623	            case "tan", True: return bc.encode_TanOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
624	            case "tanh", True: return bc.encode_TanHOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x,
625	                                                       rounding_mode=rounding_mode)
626	            case "log", True: return bc.encode_LogOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
627	            case "log2", True: return bc.encode_Log2Op(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
628	            case "sqrt", True: return bc.encode_SqrtOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x,
629	                                                       rounding_mode=rounding_mode,
630	                                                       flush_to_zero=flush_to_zero)
631	            case "rsqrt", True: return bc.encode_RsqrtOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x,
632	                                                         flush_to_zero=flush_to_zero)
633	            case "floor", True: return bc.encode_FloorOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
634	            case "ceil", True: return bc.encode_CeilOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x)
635	            case "invert" | "bitwise_not", False:
636	                # ~tx == tx ^ ~0
637	                all_ones = (-1 if datatype.is_signed(input_dtype)
638	                            else ~(-1 << input_dtype.bitwidth))
639	                all_ones_tile = ctx.constant(all_ones, input_type)
640	                return bc.encode_XOrIOp(ctx.builder, res_type_id, x, all_ones_tile)
641	            case _:
642	                raise NotImplementedError(f"Missing implementation for unary op: {self.fn}")
643	
644	
645	def _unary_promote_to_int(x):
646	    return astype(x, datatype.default_int_type)

649	def _unary_promote_to_float(x):
650	    return astype(x, datatype.default_float_type)

653	def _unary_preserve(x):
654	    return x
