657	@dataclass
658	class UnaryBehavior:
659	    bool_handler: Callable[[Var], Var] | None
660	    int_handler: Callable[[Var], Var] | None
661	    float_handler: Callable[[Var], Var] | None
662	
663	
664	def _unary_propagate_constant(fn: str, arg: Any) -> Any:
665	    impl = UNARYOP_REGISTRY[fn].impl
666	    with reraise_tile_exception():
667	        return impl(arg)
668	
669	
670	def unary(fn: str, behavior: UnaryBehavior, x: Var[TensorLikeTy],
671	          rounding_mode: RoundingMode | None = None, flush_to_zero: bool = False) -> Var:
672	    x_type = x.get_loose_type()
673	    if isinstance(x_type, LooselyTypedScalar):
674	        res = _unary_propagate_constant(fn, x_type.value)
675	        return loosely_typed_const(res)
676	
677	    input_dtype = x_type.tensor_dtype()
678	    if is_boolean(input_dtype):
679	        if behavior.bool_handler is None:
680	            raise TileTypeError("Boolean inputs are not supported")
681	        x = behavior.bool_handler(x)
682	    elif is_integral(input_dtype):
683	        if behavior.int_handler is None:
684	            raise TileTypeError("Integer inputs are not supported")
685	        x = behavior.int_handler(x)
686	    elif is_float(input_dtype):
687	        if behavior.float_handler is None:
688	            raise TileTypeError("Float inputs are not supported")
689	        x = behavior.float_handler(x)
690	    else:
691	        raise TileTypeError(f"Unexpected input dtype {input_dtype}")
692	
693	    ty = x.get_type()
694	    if x.is_constant():
695	        res = _unary_propagate_constant(fn, x.get_constant())
696	        return strictly_typed_const(res, ty)
697	
698	    # FIXME: remove cutile-specific check
699	    check_rd_and_ftz(fn, rounding_mode, flush_to_zero, ty.tensor_dtype())
700	
701	    return add_operation(Unary, ty, fn=fn, operand=x,
702	                         rounding_mode=rounding_mode, flush_to_zero=flush_to_zero)
703	
704	
705	UNARY_FLOAT = UnaryBehavior(_unary_promote_to_float, _unary_promote_to_float, _unary_preserve)
706	UNARY_STRICT_FLOAT = UnaryBehavior(None, None, _unary_preserve)
707	UNARY_INT_FLOAT = UnaryBehavior(_unary_promote_to_int, _unary_preserve, _unary_preserve)
708	UNARY_BOOL_INT = UnaryBehavior(_unary_preserve, _unary_preserve, None)
709	UNARY_ANYTHING = UnaryBehavior(_unary_preserve, _unary_preserve, _unary_preserve)
710	
711	
712	@impl(operator.invert, fixed_args=["invert", UNARY_BOOL_INT], overload=(TensorLikeTy,))
713	@impl(operator.neg, fixed_args=["neg", UNARY_INT_FLOAT], overload=(TensorLikeTy,))
714	def _invert_neg_tensorlike_impl(fn: str, behavior: UnaryBehavior, x: Var) -> Var:
715	    return unary(fn, behavior, x)
716	
717	
718	@impl(operator.not_, overload=(TensorLikeTy,))
719	def logical_not_impl(x: Var[TensorLikeTy]) -> Var[TensorLikeTy]:
720	    ty = x.get_loose_type()
721	    if isinstance(ty, LooselyTypedScalar):
722	        return loosely_typed_const(not ty.value)
723	
724	    x = astype(x, datatype.bool_)
725	    if x.is_constant():
726	        return strictly_typed_const(not x.get_constant(), x.get_type())
727	
728	    return add_operation(Unary, x.get_type(), fn="invert", operand=x,
729	                         rounding_mode=None, flush_to_zero=False)
730	
731	
732	@impl(operator.pos, overload=(TensorLikeTy,))
733	def pos_impl(x: Var[TensorLikeTy]):
734	    ty = x.get_loose_type()
735	
736	    if isinstance(ty, LooselyTypedScalar):
737	        return loosely_typed_const(+ty.value)
738	
739	    if ty.tensor_dtype() == datatype.bool_:
740	        return astype(x, datatype.default_int_type)
741	    else:
742	        return x

162	    ty = manager.get_type()

166	    state = ty.get_context_manager_state()
167	    if state.lifecycle != ContextManagerLifecycle.FRESH:
168	        raise TileTypeError("Context manager cannot be reused")
169	    state.lifecycle = ContextManagerLifecycle.ENTERED

171	
172	    try:
173	        yield (type(ty),)
174	    except OverloadNotFoundError:
175	        raise TileTypeError(f"Object of type {ty} cannot be used as a context manager")

190	def _is_none_compare(x: Var, y: Var, *, negate: bool, op_name: str) -> Var:
191	    x_is_none = x.get_type() is NONE
192	    y_is_none = y.get_type() is NONE
193	    if not (x_is_none or y_is_none):
194	        raise TileTypeError(f"Operator '{op_name}' expects one of the operands to be None")
195	    return loosely_typed_const((x_is_none == y_is_none) ^ negate)

199	class TypedConst(Operation, opcode="typed_const"):
200	    value: Any = attribute()
201	
202	    @override
203	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> bc.Value:
204	        return ctx.constant(self.value, ctx.typeof(self.result_var))

207	def loosely_typed_const(value: Any,
208	                        ty: Optional[Type] = None,
209	                        loose_ty: Optional[Type] = None,
210	                        name: str | None = None) -> Var:
211	    builder = Builder.get_current()
212	    if ty is None:
213	        ty = type_of_constant_python_value(value, builder.ir_ctx.typing_hooks)
214	    assert not ty.is_aggregate(), "Use sym2var(value, constant_only=True) instead"
215	
216	    # Normalize third party dtype spec objects (e.g. torch.float32 -> ct.float32)
217	    if isinstance(ty, DTypeSpec):
218	        value = ty.dtype
219	
220	    ret = _strictly_typed_const_inner(builder, value, ty, name=name)
221	    if loose_ty is None:
222	        loose_ty = loose_type_of_constant_python_value(value, builder.ir_ctx.typing_hooks)
223	    ret.set_loose_type(loose_ty)
224	    return ret
225	
226	
227	def strictly_typed_const(value: Any, ty: Type, name: str | None = None) -> Var:
228	    return _strictly_typed_const_inner(Builder.get_current(), value, ty, name)
229	
230	
231	def _map_nested_tuple(func, value):
232	    return (tuple(_map_nested_tuple(func, x) for x in value)
233	            if isinstance(value, tuple) else func(value))
234	
235	
236	def _strictly_typed_const_inner(builder: Builder,
237	                                value: Any, ty: Type, name: str | None = None) -> Var:
238	    if isinstance(ty, TensorLikeTy):
239	        dtype = ty.tensor_dtype()
240	        if is_integral(dtype):
241	            mask = -1 << dtype.bitwidth
242	
243	            def truncate(x):
244	                x = int(x) & ~mask
245	                assert x >= 0
246	                if datatype.is_signed(dtype) and (x >> (dtype.bitwidth - 1)):
247	                    # High bit set? Need to sign-extend it.
248	                    x |= mask
249	                    assert x < 0
250	                return x
251	
252	            value = _map_nested_tuple(truncate, value)
253	        elif datatype.is_float(dtype):
254	            bc_type = datatype.dtype_simple_bytecode_type(dtype)
255	
256	            def round_float(x):
257	                x = float(x)
258	                try:
259	                    bits = float_to_bits(x, bc_type)
260	                except ValueError as e:
261	                    raise TileValueError(str(e))
262	                return float_from_bits(bits, bc_type)
263	
264	            value = _map_nested_tuple(round_float, value)
265	
266	    result = None if name is None else builder.ir_ctx.make_var(name, builder.loc)
267	    ret = builder.add_operation(TypedConst, ty, dict(value=value), result=result)
268	    if not isinstance(ty, TensorLikeTy) or ty.tensor_shape() == ():
269	        # We currently don't have a way to represent an N-dimensional tile constant
270	        ret.set_constant(value)
271	    return ret
272

277	def builtin_numeric_ctor_impl(ctor_obj: Any, x: Var) -> Var:
278	    if not x.is_constant():
279	        raise TileTypeError(f"{ctor_obj.__name__}() expects a constant argument")
280	    const = x.get_constant()
281	    try:
282	        value = ctor_obj(const)
283	    except (ValueError, TypeError, OverflowError):
284	        raise TileTypeError(f"Invalid argument for {ctor_obj.__name__}({const})")
285	    return loosely_typed_const(value)

348	@comparison_operator_impl(_registry, TupleTy, TupleTy)
349	async def comparison_operator_tuple_impl(fn: str, x: Var[TupleTy], y: Var[TupleTy]) -> Var:
350	    if fn not in ("eq", "ne"):
351	        raise TileTypeError(f"Operator '{fn}' is not supported for tuples")
352	
353	    x_ty = x.get_type()
354	    y_ty = y.get_type()
355	
356	    if x.is_constant() and y.is_constant():
357	        res = x.get_constant() == y.get_constant()
358	        return loosely_typed_const(res if fn == "eq" else not res)
359	
360	    if len(x_ty) != len(y_ty):
361	        return loosely_typed_const(fn == "ne")
362	
363	    x_items = x.get_aggregate().items
364	    y_items = y.get_aggregate().items
365	
366	    for item in (*x_items, *y_items):
367	        item_ty = item.get_type()
368	        if isinstance(item_ty, TensorLikeTy) and len(item_ty.tensor_shape()) > 0:
369	            raise TileTypeError("Tuple comparison is not supported for non-scalar elements")
370	        if not isinstance(item_ty, (TensorLikeTy, TupleTy, DTypeSpec, StringTy)):
371	            raise TileTypeError(
372	                f"Tuple comparison is not supported for elements of type {item_ty}"
373	            )
374	
375	    from cuda.tile._passes.hir2ir import call_function
376	    from cuda.tile._ir.arithmetic_ops import binary_bitwise_tensorlike
377	    elem_cmps = [await call_function(operator.eq, xi, yi) for xi, yi in zip(x_items, y_items)]
378	    result = functools.reduce(lambda a, b: binary_bitwise_tensorlike("and_", a, b), elem_cmps,
379	                              loosely_typed_const(True))
380	
381	    if fn == "ne":
382	        from cuda.tile._ir.arithmetic_ops import logical_not_impl
383	        result = logical_not_impl(result)
384	
385	    return result
386

393	@impl(hir_stubs.is_contained_in, overload=(WILDCARD, TupleTy))
394	async def is_contained_in_tuple_impl(x: Var, y: Var[TupleTy]) -> Var:
395	    from cuda.tile._passes.hir2ir import call_function
396	    from cuda.tile._ir.arithmetic_ops import binary_bitwise_tensorlike
397	
398	    tuple_val = y.get_aggregate()
399	    assert isinstance(tuple_val, TupleValue)
400	    items = tuple_val.items
401	
402	    result = None
403	    for item in items:
404	        cmp = await call_function(operator.eq, x, item)
405	        cmp_ty = cmp.get_type()
406	        if isinstance(cmp_ty, TensorLikeTy) and cmp_ty.tensor_shape() != ():
407	            raise TileTypeError(
408	                f"'in' requires scalar operands, but got shape {cmp_ty.tensor_shape()}")
409	        if cmp.is_constant() and cmp.get_constant():
410	            return cmp
411	        result = cmp if result is None else binary_bitwise_tensorlike("or_", result, cmp)
412	    return loosely_typed_const(False) if result is None else result

419	def build_dict(keys: tuple[str, ...], values: tuple[Var, ...]) -> Var:
420	    keys = tuple(keys)

484	def build_dataclass_instance(items: tuple[Var, ...], info: DataclassInfo) -> Var:
485	    cls = info.cls
486	    ty = DataclassTy(cls, tuple(x.get_type() for x in items))
487	    loose_ty = DataclassTy(cls, tuple(x.get_loose_type() for x in items))
488	    res = make_aggregate(DataclassValue(items, info), ty, loose_ty)
489	    if all(x.is_constant() for x in items):
490	        const_val = cls(**{name: x.get_constant()
491	                           for name, x in zip(info.field_names, items, strict=True)})
492	        res.set_constant(const_val)
493	    return res

497	def dataclasses_replace_impl(obj: Var, changes: dict[str, Var]):
498	    dataclass_ty = require_dataclass_type(obj)
499	    dataclass_val = obj.get_aggregate()
500	    assert isinstance(dataclass_val, DataclassValue)
501	    name2idx = dataclass_val.info.field_name_to_idx
502	    new_items = list(dataclass_val.items)
503	    for name, val in changes.items():
504	        try:
505	            idx = name2idx[name]
506	        except KeyError:
507	            raise TileTypeError(f"Dataclass '{dataclass_ty.cls.__name__}'"
508	                                f" has no such field '{name}'")
509	        new_items[idx] = val
510	    return build_dataclass_instance(tuple(new_items), dataclass_val.info)

521	def sym2var(x: Any, constant_only: bool = False) -> Var:
522	    # TODO: verify we don't have a stale closure
523	
524	    if isinstance(x, Symbol):
525	        if constant_only:
526	            raise TileTypeError("Cannot create a constant from a symbolic value")
527	        return x._var
528	
529	    if isinstance(x, tuple):
530	        return build_tuple(tuple(sym2var(item, constant_only=constant_only) for item in x))
531	
532	    cls = type(x)
533	    if dataclasses.is_dataclass(cls):
534	        info = get_dataclass_info(cls)
535	        field_vars = tuple(sym2var(getattr(x, f.name), constant_only=constant_only)
536	                           for f in dataclasses.fields(cls))
537	        return build_dataclass_instance(field_vars, info)
538	
539	    if isinstance(x, MethodType):
540	        self_var = sym2var(x.__self__, constant_only=constant_only)
541	        if not isinstance(x.__func__, FunctionType | BuiltinFunctionType):
542	            raise TileTypeError(f"Object of type {type(x).__name__}"
543	                                f" cannot be used as a function for binding a method")
544	        return bind_method(self_var, x.__func__)
545	
546	    # Transform a third party typed scalar (e.g., np.int16(5)) into a strictly typed constant
547	    dtype_spec = as_third_party_dtype_spec(type(x))
548	    if dtype_spec is not None:
549	        pyval = numeric_dtype_category(dtype_spec.dtype).pytype(x)
550	        ty = Builder.get_current().ir_ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(dtype_spec.dtype, ())
551	        return strictly_typed_const(pyval, ty)
552	
553	    return loosely_typed_const(x)
554

627	@impl(hir_stubs.make_closure)
628	def make_closure_impl(func_hir: hir.Function, default_values: tuple[Var, ...]):
629	    default_value_types = tuple(v.get_type() for v in default_values)
630	
631	    frozen_captures_by_depth = []
632	    frozen_capture_types_by_depth = []
633	    captured_scopes = []
634	
635	    builder = Builder.get_current()
636	    scope = Scope.get_current()
637	    for depth, (local_scope, captured_indices) in (enumerate(
638	            zip(scope.local_scopes, func_hir.captures_by_depth, strict=True))):
639	        if local_scope.frozen:
640	            frozen_vars = tuple(local_scope.get(idx, builder.loc) for idx in captured_indices)
641	            frozen_captures_by_depth.append(frozen_vars)
642	            frozen_types = tuple(v.get_type_allow_invalid() for v in frozen_vars)
643	            frozen_capture_types_by_depth.append(frozen_types)
644	        else:
645	            captured_scopes.append(LiveCapturedScope(depth, local_scope))
646	            frozen_captures_by_depth.append(None)
647	            frozen_capture_types_by_depth.append(None)
648	
649	    closure_ty = ClosureTy(func_hir, default_value_types, tuple(captured_scopes),
650	                           tuple(frozen_capture_types_by_depth))
651	    closure_val = ClosureValue(default_values, tuple(frozen_captures_by_depth))
652	    return make_aggregate(closure_val, closure_ty)

680	async def getattr_dataclass_impl(object: Var, name: Var):
681	    ty = object.get_type()
682	    val = object.get_aggregate()
683	    assert isinstance(val, DataclassValue)
684	    attr_name = require_constant_str(name)
685	    field_idx = val.info.field_name_to_idx.get(attr_name)
686	    if field_idx is not None:

689	    cls = ty.cls
690	    try:

694	
695	    if isinstance(cls_attr, FunctionType | BuiltinFunctionType):
696	        return bind_method(object, cls_attr)
697	    elif isinstance(cls_attr, property):
698	        from .._passes.hir2ir import call
699	        getter = loosely_typed_const(cls_attr.fget)
700	        return await call(getter, (object,), {})
701	    else:
702	        return sym2var(cls_attr, constant_only=True)

717	@impl(range)
718	def range_(args: tuple[Var, ...]) -> Var:
719	    if not 1 <= len(args) <= 3:
720	        raise TileTypeError(f"Invalid number of arguments: {len(args)}")
721	    for arg in args:
722	        require_signed_integer_scalar_type(arg)
723	
724	    get_tensor_ty = args[0].ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type
725	
726	    if len(args) == 1:
727	        start = strictly_typed_const(0, get_tensor_ty(datatype.default_int_type, ()))
728	        stop = args[0]
729	        step = strictly_typed_const(1, get_tensor_ty(datatype.default_int_type, ()))
730	    elif len(args) == 2:
731	        start, stop = args[0], args[1]
732	        step = strictly_typed_const(1, get_tensor_ty(datatype.default_int_type, ()))
733	    else:
734	        start, stop, step = args[0], args[1], args[2]
735	        # FIXME(Issue 314): Support negative step.
736	        # Error out if step is constant and not positive.
737	        if step.is_constant() and step.get_constant() <= 0:
738	            raise TileTypeError(f"Step must be positive, got {step.get_constant()}")
739	
740	    agg_value = RangeValue(start, stop, step)
741	    ty = RangeIterType(datatype.default_int_type)
742	    return make_aggregate(agg_value, ty)
743

745	@impl(hir_stubs.unpack)
746	def unpack_impl(iterable: Var, expected_len: Var) -> Var:
747	    ty = iterable.get_type()
748	    # Don't use the require_tuple_type() helper because we'd like to customize the error message
749	    if not isinstance(ty, TupleTy):
750	        raise TileTypeError("Expected a tuple", iterable.loc)
751	    expected_len = require_constant_int(expected_len)
752	    if len(ty.value_types) != expected_len:
753	        few_many = "few" if len(ty.value_types) < expected_len else "many"
754	        raise TileValueError(f"Too {few_many} values to unpack"
755	                             f" (expected {expected_len}, got {len(ty.value_types)})")
756	    # Return the input tuple. If we add support for additional iterables,
757	    # the idea is to cast them to a tuple here.
758	    return iterable

783	@dataclass(eq=False)
784	class TilePrintf(Operation, opcode="tile_printf", memory_effect=MemoryEffect.STORE):
785	    format: str = attribute()
786	    args: tuple[Var, ...] = operand()
787	    token: Optional[Var] = operand(default=None)
788	
789	    @override
790	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext):
791	        arg_vars = [ctx.get_value(arg) for arg in self.args]
792	        token = None if self.token is None else ctx.get_value(self.token)
793	        if ctx.builder.version >= bc.BytecodeVersion.V_13_2:
794	            result_typeid = ctx.type_table.Token
795	            return bc.encode_PrintTkoOp(ctx.builder, result_typeid, arg_vars, token, self.format)
796	        else:
797	            with tile_mutex("print_mutex", ctx):
798	                result_typeid = None
799	                bc.encode_PrintTkoOp(ctx.builder, result_typeid, arg_vars, None, self.format)
800	
801	                # Bytecode < 13.2 does not produce or consume print ordering tokens.
802	                # Return a dummy only to satisfy the IR result_var mapping.
803	                return bc.encode_MakeTokenOp(ctx.builder, ctx.type_table.Token)
804	
805	
806	@impl(print)
807	def print_impl(args: tuple[Var, ...], sep: Var, end: Var) -> None:
808	    format_parts = []
809	    leaf_vars = []
810	
811	    def _get_string_quotes(has_single_quote: bool, has_double_quote: bool) -> str:
812	        return "'" if not has_single_quote or has_double_quote else '"'
813	
814	    def _expand_var(var: Var | str, format_spec: str | None = None,
815	                    is_tuple_element: bool = False, escape_in_str: str | None = None):
816	        if isinstance(var, str) or isinstance(ty := var.get_type(), StringTy):
817	            str_val = var if isinstance(var, str) else ty.value
818	            escaped = PrintfValidator.escape_str(str_val)
819	            if is_tuple_element:
820	                string_quote = _get_string_quotes("'" in escaped, '"' in escaped)
821	                escaped = escaped.replace(string_quote, f"\\{string_quote}")
822	                format_parts.append(f"{string_quote}{escaped}{string_quote}")
823	            else:
824	                if escape_in_str is not None:
825	                    escaped = escaped.replace(escape_in_str, f"\\{escape_in_str}")
826	                format_parts.append(escaped)
827	        elif isinstance(ty, FormattedStringTy):
828	            if is_tuple_element:
829	                string_quote = _get_string_quotes(ty.has_single_quote, ty.has_double_quote)
830	                format_parts.append(string_quote)
831	            else:
832	                string_quote = None
833	            val = var.get_aggregate()
834	            for piece in ty.format.pieces:
835	                if isinstance(piece, str):
836	                    _expand_var(piece, escape_in_str=string_quote)
837	                else:
838	                    _expand_var(val.values[piece.value_idx], piece.format_spec,
839	                                escape_in_str=string_quote)
840	            if is_tuple_element:
841	                format_parts.append(string_quote)
842	        elif isinstance(ty, TupleTy):
843	            if format_spec is not None:
844	                raise TileTypeError(
845	                    "f-string: cannot apply format spec to a tuple value",
846	                    var.loc)
847	
848	            agg = var.get_aggregate()
849	            format_parts.append('(')
850	            for i, item in enumerate(agg.items):
851	                _expand_var(item, is_tuple_element=True)
852	                if i < len(agg.items) - 1:
853	                    format_parts.append(', ')
854	            format_parts.append(',)' if len(agg.items) == 1 else ')')
855	        elif isinstance(ty, DataclassTy):
856	            if format_spec is not None:
857	                raise TileTypeError("f-string: cannot apply format spec to a dataclass instance",
858	                                    var.loc)
859	
860	            if not dataclass_has_default_formatter(ty.cls):
861	                raise TileTypeError("Printing dataclasses with custom __repr__/__str__/__format__"
862	                                    " is not supported")
863	
864	            agg = var.get_aggregate()
865	            assert isinstance(agg, DataclassValue)
866	            format_parts.append(PrintfValidator.escape_str(f"{ty.cls.__qualname__}("))
867	            comma = ""
868	            for f, item in zip(dataclasses.fields(ty.cls), agg.items, strict=True):
869	                if not f.repr:
870	                    continue
871	
872	                format_parts.append(PrintfValidator.escape_str(f"{comma}{f.name}="))
873	                _expand_var(item, is_tuple_element=True)
874	                comma = ", "
875	            format_parts.append(")")
876	        elif isinstance(ty, TensorLikeTy):
877	            if format_spec is not None:
878	                format_parts.append(PrintfValidator.apply_python_spec(
879	                    format_spec, ty.tensor_dtype()))
880	            else:
881	                format_parts.append(PrintfValidator.infer_format(ty.tensor_dtype()))
882	            leaf_vars.append(var)
883	        elif isinstance(ty, DTypeSpec):
884	            format_parts.append(str(ty.dtype))
885	        elif isinstance(ty, EnumTy):
886	            member = var.get_constant()
887	            format_parts.append(f"{ty.enum_ty.__name__}.{member.name}")
888	        else:
889	            raise TileTypeError(f"Can't print value of type {ty}")
890	
891	    for i, arg_var in enumerate(args):
892	        if i > 0:
893	            format_parts.append(PrintfValidator.escape_str(require_constant_str(sep)))
894	        _expand_var(arg_var)
895	    format_parts.append(PrintfValidator.escape_str(require_constant_str(end)))
896	
897	    final_format = ''.join(format_parts)
898	    add_operation_variadic(TilePrintf, (TokenTy(),), format=final_format, args=tuple(leaf_vars))
899	
900	
901	@impl(hir_stubs.build_formatted_string)
902	def build_formatted_string_impl(format: StringFormat, values: tuple[Var, ...]) -> Var:
903	    new_pieces = []
904	    new_values = []
905	    has_single_quote = False
906	    has_double_quote = False
907	
908	    def _update_has_quote_flags(s: str):
909	        nonlocal has_single_quote, has_double_quote
910	        if "'" in s:
911	            has_single_quote = True
912	        if '"' in s:
913	            has_double_quote = True
914	
915	    def _build_formatted_string(fmt: StringFormat, vals: tuple[Var, ...]):
916	        for piece in fmt.pieces:
917	            if isinstance(piece, str):
918	                new_pieces.append(piece)
919	                _update_has_quote_flags(piece)
920	            else:
921	                val_var = vals[piece.value_idx]
922	                val_ty = val_var.get_type()
923	                if isinstance(val_ty, FormattedStringTy):
924	                    if piece.format_spec is not None:
925	                        raise TileTypeError(
926	                            "f-string: cannot apply format spec to a formatted string value",
927	                            val_var.loc)
928	                    inner_val = val_var.get_aggregate()
929	                    assert isinstance(inner_val, FormattedStringValue)
930	                    _build_formatted_string(val_ty.format, inner_val.values)
931	                else:
932	                    new_pieces.append(FormattedPiece(len(new_values), piece.format_spec))
933	                    new_values.append(val_var)
934	                    if isinstance(val_ty, StringTy):
935	                        _update_has_quote_flags(val_ty.value)
936	
937	    _build_formatted_string(format, values)
938	    new_fmt = StringFormat(tuple(new_pieces))
939	    ty = FormattedStringTy(new_fmt, tuple(v.get_type() for v in new_values),
940	                           has_single_quote, has_double_quote)
941	    return make_aggregate(FormattedStringValue(new_fmt, tuple(new_values)), ty)
942

38	class IRContext:
39	    def __init__(self, log_ir_on_error: bool, tileiras_version: BytecodeVersion,
40	                 typing_hooks: TypingHooks):
41	        self._all_vars: Dict[str, str] = {}
42	        self._counter_by_name: Dict[str, Iterator[int]] = defaultdict(itertools.count)
43	        self._temp_counter = itertools.count()
44	        self.typemap: Dict[str, Type] = dict()
45	        self.constants: Dict[str, Any] = dict()
46	        self._loose_typemap: Dict[str, Type] = dict()
47	        self.log_ir_on_error = log_ir_on_error
48	        self._aggregate_values: Dict[str, Any] = dict()
49	        self.tileiras_version: BytecodeVersion = tileiras_version
50	        self._function_specialization_id_counter = itertools.count()
51	        self.typing_hooks = typing_hooks
52	
53	    def next_function_specialization_id(self) -> str:
54	        # Monotonic counter used as a unique id when creating concrete FunctionDescs
55	        # to distinguish them in debug info. The monotonicity ensures that the
56	        # emitted bytecode is deterministic for cache hits.
57	        return f"s{next(self._function_specialization_id_counter)}"
58	
59	    #  Make a Var with a unique name based on `name`.
60	    def make_var(self, name: str, loc: Loc) -> Var:
61	        var_name = name
62	        while var_name in self._all_vars:
63	            var_name = f"{name}.{next(self._counter_by_name[name])}"
64	        self._all_vars[var_name] = name
65	        return Var(var_name, loc, self)
66	
67	    def make_var_like(self, var: Var) -> Var:
68	        return self.make_var(self.get_original_name(var.name), var.loc)
69	
70	    def make_temp(self, loc: Loc) -> Var:
71	        return self.make_var(f"${next(self._temp_counter)}", loc)
72	
73	    def get_original_name(self, var_name: str) -> str:
74	        return self._all_vars[var_name]
75	
76	    def copy_type_information(self, src: Var, dst: Var):
77	        if src.name in self.typemap:
78	            self.typemap[dst.name] = self.typemap[src.name]
79	        if src.name in self._loose_typemap:
80	            self._loose_typemap[dst.name] = self._loose_typemap[src.name]
81	        if src.name in self.constants:
82	            self.constants[dst.name] = self.constants[src.name]
83	        if src.name in self._aggregate_values:
84	            self._aggregate_values[dst.name] = self._aggregate_values[src.name]

87	class ConstantState(enum.Enum):
88	    UNSET = 0
89	    MAY_BE_CONSTANT = 1
90	    NONCONSTANT = 2
91	
92	
93	@dataclass
94	class PhiState:
95	    ty: Type | None = None
96	    loose_ty: Type | None = None
97	    last_loc: Loc = Loc.unknown()
98	    initial_constant_state: ConstantState = ConstantState.UNSET
99	
100	    # Constant propagation state, per aggregate item.
101	    # We initialize it to None because we don't know yet how many items we have.
102	    constant_state: list[ConstantState] | None = None
103	    constant_value: list[Any] | None = None
104	
105	    def propagate(self, src: Var, fail_eagerly: bool = False, allow_loose_typing: bool = True):
106	        # Type & loose type propagation
107	        src_ty = src.get_type_allow_invalid()
108	        src_loose_ty = src.get_loose_type_allow_invalid() if allow_loose_typing else src_ty
109	        if self.ty is None:
110	            self.ty = src_ty
111	            self.loose_ty = src_loose_ty
112	            self.last_loc = src.loc
113	        elif isinstance(src_ty, InvalidType):
114	            if fail_eagerly and not isinstance(self.ty, InvalidType):
115	                raise TileTypeError(src_ty.error_message, src_ty.loc)
116	            self.ty = self.loose_ty = src_ty
117	        elif not isinstance(self.ty, InvalidType):
118	            var_name = src.get_original_name()
119	            if src_ty != self.ty:
120	                msg = (f"Type of `{var_name}` depends on path taken:"
121	                       f" {src_ty} (line {src.loc.line}) vs. {self.ty} (line {self.last_loc.line})")
122	                if fail_eagerly:
123	                    raise TileTypeError(msg, src.loc)
124	                else:
125	                    self.ty = self.loose_ty = InvalidType(msg, loc=src.loc)
126	
127	            # If the loose types don't match exactly, "unify" them to the concrete type
128	            self.last_loc = src.loc
129	            if self.loose_ty != src_loose_ty:
130	                self.loose_ty = self.ty
131	
132	        # Constant propagation
133	        if isinstance(src_ty, InvalidType):
134	            self.constant_state = None
135	            self.initial_constant_state = ConstantState.NONCONSTANT
136	        else:
137	            agg_items = tuple(src.flatten_aggregate())
138	            if self.constant_state is None:
139	                self.constant_state = [self.initial_constant_state for _ in range(len(agg_items))]
140	                self.constant_value = [None for _ in range(len(agg_items))]
141	            else:
142	                # This should be true because we already checked the type.
143	                # If the type matches, it must have the same aggregate length.
144	                assert len(self.constant_state) == len(agg_items)
145	
146	            for i, item in enumerate(agg_items):
147	                if item.is_constant():
148	                    new_const = item.get_constant()
149	                    if self.constant_state[i] == ConstantState.UNSET:
150	                        self.constant_state[i] = ConstantState.MAY_BE_CONSTANT
151	                        self.constant_value[i] = new_const
152	                    elif (self.constant_state[i] == ConstantState.MAY_BE_CONSTANT
153	                          and new_const != self.constant_value[i]):
154	                        self.constant_state[i] = ConstantState.NONCONSTANT
155	                else:
156	                    self.constant_state[i] = ConstantState.NONCONSTANT
157	
158	    def finalize_constant_and_loose_type(self, dst: Var):
159	        assert self.constant_state is not None
160	        for item, state, val in zip(dst.flatten_aggregate(),
161	                                    self.constant_state, self.constant_value, strict=True):
162	            if state == ConstantState.MAY_BE_CONSTANT:
163	                item.set_constant(val)
164	        dst.set_loose_type(self.loose_ty)
165

167	T = TypeVar("T", bound=Type, covariant=True)

169	
170	class Var(Generic[T]):
171	    def __init__(self, name: str, loc: Loc, ctx: IRContext):
172	        self.name = name
173	        self.loc = loc
174	        self.ctx = ctx
175	
176	    def try_get_type(self) -> Optional[T]:
177	        return self.ctx.typemap.get(self.name)
178	
179	    def get_type(self) -> T:
180	        ty = self.get_type_allow_invalid()
181	        if isinstance(ty, InvalidType):
182	            raise TileTypeError(ty.error_message, ty.loc)
183	        return ty
184	
185	    def get_type_allow_invalid(self) -> T | InvalidType:
186	        try:
187	            return self.ctx.typemap[self.name]
188	        except KeyError:
189	            raise TileInternalError(f"Type of variable {self.name} not found")
190	
191	    def set_type(self, ty: Type, force: bool = False):
192	        assert isinstance(ty, Type)
193	        if not force:
194	            assert self.name not in self.ctx.typemap
195	        self.ctx.typemap[self.name] = ty
196	
197	    def is_constant(self) -> bool:
198	        return self.name in self.ctx.constants
199	
200	    def get_constant(self):
201	        return self.ctx.constants[self.name]
202	
203	    def set_constant(self, value):
204	        assert self.name not in self.ctx.constants
205	        self.ctx.constants[self.name] = value
206	
207	    def get_loose_type(self) -> T | LooselyTypedScalar:
208	        ty = self.ctx._loose_typemap.get(self.name, None)
209	        if ty is not None:
210	            return ty
211	        ty = self.get_type()
212	        if isinstance(ty, InvalidType):
213	            raise TileTypeError(ty.error_message, ty.loc)
214	        return ty
215	
216	    def get_loose_type_allow_invalid(self) -> T | LooselyTypedScalar | InvalidType:
217	        ty = self.ctx._loose_typemap.get(self.name, None)
218	        return self.get_type_allow_invalid() if ty is None else ty
219	
220	    def set_loose_type(self, ty: Type, force: bool = False):
221	        assert isinstance(ty, Type)
222	        if not force:
223	            assert self.name not in self.ctx._loose_typemap
224	        self.ctx._loose_typemap[self.name] = ty
225	
226	    def get_original_name(self) -> str:
227	        return self.ctx.get_original_name(self.name)
228	
229	    def is_aggregate(self) -> bool:
230	        return self.name in self.ctx._aggregate_values
231	
232	    def get_aggregate(self) -> AggregateValue:
233	        return self.ctx._aggregate_values[self.name]
234	
235	    def set_aggregate(self, agg_value: AggregateValue):
236	        self.ctx._aggregate_values[self.name] = agg_value
237	
238	    def flatten_aggregate(self) -> Iterator["Var"]:
239	        if self.is_aggregate():
240	            for x in self.get_aggregate().as_tuple():
241	                yield from x.flatten_aggregate()
242	        else:
243	            yield self
244	
245	    def __repr__(self):
246	        return f"Var<{self.name} @{self.loc}>"
247	
248	    def __str__(self) -> str:
249	        return self.name
250

269	class Mapper:
270	    def __init__(self, ctx: IRContext, preserve_vars: bool = False):
271	        self._ctx = ctx
272	        self._var_map: Dict[str, Var] = dict()
273	        self._preserve_vars = preserve_vars
274	
275	    def is_empty(self):
276	        return len(self._var_map) == 0
277	
278	    def clone_var(self, var: Var) -> Var:
279	        if self._preserve_vars:
280	            return self.get_var(var)
281	        else:
282	            new_var = self._ctx.make_var_like(var)
283	            self._var_map[var.name] = new_var
284	            self._ctx.copy_type_information(var, new_var)
285	            return new_var
286	
287	    def clone_vars(self, vars: Sequence[Var]) -> Tuple[Var, ...]:
288	        return tuple(self.clone_var(v) for v in vars)
289	
290	    def get_var(self, old_var: Var) -> Var:
291	        return self._var_map.get(old_var.name, old_var)
292	
293	    def set_var(self, old_var: Var, new_var: Var):
294	        assert old_var.name not in self._var_map
295	        self._var_map[old_var.name] = new_var

314	@dataclass
315	class LoopVarState:
316	    body_phi: PhiState
317	    result_phi: PhiState
318	
319	    def finalize_loopvar_type(self, body_var: Var):
320	        # Body type should always be populated from the initial value's type.
321	        assert self.body_phi.ty is not None
322	
323	        # "While" loop without a "break" statement. Should perhaps be rejected as an infinite loop,
324	        # but this is not this function's responsibility. Just use the body variable's type.
325	        if self.result_phi.ty is None:
326	            return
327	
328	        # Ignore Invalid types (unless both are Invalid, in which case we preserve one of them).
329	        if isinstance(self.result_phi.ty, InvalidType):
330	            pass
331	        elif isinstance(self.body_phi.ty, InvalidType):
332	            # It is OK to overwrite the body variable's type because the loop body has
333	            # already been type-inferred by this point.
334	            body_var.set_type(self.result_phi.ty, force=True)
335	            body_var.set_loose_type(self.result_phi.loose_ty, force=True)
336	        elif self.body_phi.ty != self.result_phi.ty:
337	            # TODO: split the variable into two to actually support this case?
338	            var_name = body_var.get_original_name()
339	            raise TileTypeError(f"Variable {var_name} has changed its type inside a loop"
340	                                f" from {self.body_phi.ty} (line {self.body_phi.last_loc.line})"
341	                                f" to {self.result_phi.ty} (line {self.result_phi.last_loc.line}")
342

344	class Builder:
345	    def __init__(self, ctx: IRContext, loc: Loc,
346	                 block_restriction: Optional[BlockRestriction] = None):
347	        self.ir_ctx = ctx
348	        self.is_terminated = False
349	        self._loc = loc
350	        self._ops = []
351	        self._entered = False
352	        self._prev_builder = None
353	        self._var_map: Dict[str, Var] = dict()
354	        self.block_restriction = block_restriction
355	
356	    def add_operation(self, op_class,
357	                      result_ty: T,
358	                      attrs_and_operands: Mapping,
359	                      result: Var | None = None) -> Var[T]:
360	        assert isinstance(result_ty, Type)
361	        return self._add_operation(op_class, result_ty, attrs_and_operands, result)
362	
363	    def add_operation_variadic(self, op_class,
364	                               result_types: tuple[Type, ...],
365	                               attrs_and_operands: Mapping,
366	                               result: Var | None = None) -> tuple[Var, ...]:
367	        assert isinstance(result_types, tuple)
368	        return self._add_operation(op_class, result_types, attrs_and_operands, result)
369	
370	    def _add_operation(self, op_class,
371	                       result_ty: Type | None | Tuple[Type | None, ...],
372	                       attrs_and_operands: Mapping,
373	                       result: Var | Sequence[Var] | None = None) -> Var | Tuple[Var, ...]:
374	        if self.block_restriction is not None:
375	            self.block_restriction.validate_operation(op_class)
376	
377	        assert not self.is_terminated
378	        force_type = False
379	        if isinstance(result_ty, tuple):
380	            if result is None:
381	                result = tuple(self.ir_ctx.make_temp(self._loc) for _ in result_ty)
382	            else:
383	                result = tuple(result)
384	                assert all(isinstance(v, Var) for v in result)
385	                force_type = True
386	
387	            for var, ty in zip(result, result_ty, strict=True):
388	                if ty is not None:
389	                    var.set_type(ty, force=force_type)
390	
391	            result_vars = result
392	        else:
393	            if result is None:
394	                result = self.ir_ctx.make_temp(self._loc)
395	            else:
396	                assert isinstance(result, Var)
397	                force_type = True
398	            if result_ty is not None:
399	                result.set_type(result_ty, force=force_type)
400	
401	            result_vars = (result,)
402	
403	        new_op = op_class(**attrs_and_operands, loc=self._loc, result_vars=result_vars)
404	        self._ops.append(new_op)
405	        if new_op.is_terminator:
406	            self.is_terminated = True
407	        return result
408	
409	    def make_aggregate(self,
410	                       value: AggregateValue,
411	                       ty: Type | None,
412	                       loose_ty: Type | None = None,
413	                       result_var: Var | None = None) -> Var:
414	        force_type = True
415	        if result_var is None:
416	            result_var = self.ir_ctx.make_temp(self._loc)
417	            force_type = False
418	
419	        if ty is not None:
420	            result_var.set_type(ty, force=force_type)
421	        if loose_ty is not None:
422	            result_var.set_loose_type(ty, force=force_type)
423	        result_var.set_aggregate(value)
424	        return result_var
425	
426	    @property
427	    def ops(self) -> list[Operation]:
428	        return self._ops
429	
430	    @property
431	    def loc(self) -> Loc:
432	        return self._loc
433	
434	    def append_verbatim(self, op: Operation):
435	        self._ops.append(op)
436	
437	    def extend_verbatim(self, ops: Sequence[Operation]):
438	        self._ops.extend(ops)
439	
440	    @staticmethod
441	    def get_current() -> "Builder":
442	        ret = _current_builder.builder
443	        assert ret is not None, "No IR builder is currently active"
444	        return ret
445	
446	    @contextmanager
447	    def change_loc(self, loc: Loc):
448	        old_loc = self._loc
449	        self._loc = loc
450	        try:
451	            yield
452	        finally:
453	            self._loc = old_loc
454	
455	    def __enter__(self):
456	        assert not self._entered
457	        self._prev_builder = _current_builder.builder
458	        _current_builder.builder = self
459	        self._entered = True
460	        return self
461	
462	    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
463	        assert self._entered
464	        _current_builder.builder = self._prev_builder
465	        self._prev_builder = None
466	        self._entered = False
467	
468	
469	@contextmanager
470	def enter_nested_block(loc: Loc, block_restriction: Optional[BlockRestriction] = None):
471	    prev_builder = Builder.get_current()
472	    block = Block(prev_builder.ir_ctx, loc=loc)
473	    with Builder(prev_builder.ir_ctx, loc,
474	                 block_restriction=block_restriction or prev_builder.block_restriction) as builder:
475	        yield block
476	    block.extend(builder.ops)
477	
478	
479	class _CurrentBuilder(threading.local):
480	    builder = None
481	
482	
483	_current_builder = _CurrentBuilder()

486	class _FieldKind(enum.IntEnum):
487	    OPERAND = 0
488	    ATTRIBUTE = 1
489	    NESTED_BLOCK = 2
490	
491	
492	_FIELD_METADATA_KEY = "operation_field_kind"
493	
494	
495	def attribute(*, default=dataclasses.MISSING) -> dataclasses.Field:
496	    return dataclasses.field(default=default, metadata={_FIELD_METADATA_KEY: _FieldKind.ATTRIBUTE},
497	                             kw_only=True)
498	
499	
500	def operand(*, default=dataclasses.MISSING) -> dataclasses.Field:
501	    return dataclasses.field(default=default, metadata={_FIELD_METADATA_KEY: _FieldKind.OPERAND},
502	                             kw_only=True)
503	
504	
505	def nested_block() -> dataclasses.Field:
506	    return dataclasses.field(metadata={_FIELD_METADATA_KEY: _FieldKind.NESTED_BLOCK},
507	                             kw_only=True)
508	
509	
510	def _get_result_vars_tuple_for_single_result_op(self):
511	    return (self.result_var,)

514	@dataclass(eq=False)
515	class Operation:
516	    result_vars: tuple[Var, ...]
517	    loc: Loc
518	
519	    def __init_subclass__(cls,
520	                          opcode: str,
521	                          terminator: bool = False,
522	                          memory_effect: MemoryEffect = MemoryEffect.NONE):
523	        cls._opcode = opcode
524	        cls._is_terminator = terminator
525	        cls.memory_effect = memory_effect
526	
527	        operand_names = []
528	        attribute_names = []
529	        nested_block_names = []
530	        for field_name in cls.__annotations__.keys():
531	            f = getattr(cls, field_name, None)
532	            kind = f.metadata.get(_FIELD_METADATA_KEY) if isinstance(f, dataclasses.Field) else None
533	            if kind == _FieldKind.OPERAND:
534	                operand_names.append(field_name)
535	            elif kind == _FieldKind.ATTRIBUTE:
536	                attribute_names.append(field_name)
537	            elif kind == _FieldKind.NESTED_BLOCK:
538	                nested_block_names.append(field_name)
539	            else:
540	                raise TypeError(f"Field {field_name} of {cls} must be annotated with either"
541	                                f" operand(), attribute() or nested_block()")
542	
543	        cls._operand_names = tuple(operand_names)
544	        cls._attribute_names = tuple(attribute_names)
545	        cls._nested_block_names = tuple(nested_block_names)
546	
547	    def __post_init__(self):
548	        for var in self.all_inputs():
549	            assert isinstance(var, Var | tuple) or var is None
550	            if isinstance(var, tuple):
551	                assert all(isinstance(x, Var) for x in var)
552	
553	            if isinstance(var, Var) and var.is_aggregate() and self.op != "assign":
554	                # Don't allow aggregate values as operands, except for arrays and lists.
555	                # All other aggregates should only exist in the HIR level.
556	                # Also make an exception for the Assign op, until we find a better way to handle it.
557	                agg_val = var.get_aggregate()
558	                from .type import ArrayValue, ListValue
559	                assert isinstance(agg_val, ArrayValue | ListValue)
560	
561	        for nb in self.nested_blocks:
562	            assert isinstance(nb, Block)
563	
564	    def clone(self, mapper: Mapper) -> Operation:
565	        result_vars = mapper.clone_vars(self.result_vars)
566	        return self._clone_impl(mapper, result_vars)
567	
568	    def _clone_impl(self, mapper: Mapper, result_vars: Sequence[Var]) -> Operation:
569	        new_fields = {}
570	
571	        for name in self._attribute_names:
572	            new_fields[name] = getattr(self, name)
573	
574	        for name in self._operand_names:
575	            var = getattr(self, name)
576	            if isinstance(var, Var):
577	                new_var = mapper.get_var(var)
578	            elif var is None:
579	                new_var = None
580	            else:
581	                new_var = tuple(mapper.get_var(v) for v in var)
582	            new_fields[name] = new_var
583	
584	        for name in self._nested_block_names:
585	            old_block = getattr(self, name)
586	            new_block = Block(old_block.ctx, old_block.loc)
587	            new_block.params = mapper.clone_vars(old_block.params)
588	            for old_op in old_block:
589	                new_block.append(old_op.clone(mapper))
590	            new_fields[name] = new_block
591	
592	        return type(self)(result_vars=tuple(result_vars), loc=self.loc, **new_fields)
593	
594	    @property
595	    def op(self) -> str:
596	        return self._opcode
597	
598	    @property
599	    def operands(self) -> Mapping[str, Var | Tuple[Var, ...]]:
600	        return MappingProxyType({name: getattr(self, name) for name in self._operand_names})
601	
602	    @property
603	    def attributes(self):
604	        return MappingProxyType({name: getattr(self, name) for name in self._attribute_names})
605	
606	    @property
607	    def nested_blocks(self):
608	        return tuple(getattr(self, name) for name in self._nested_block_names)
609	
610	    def all_inputs(self) -> Iterator[Var]:
611	        for name in self._operand_names:
612	            x = getattr(self, name)
613	            if isinstance(x, tuple):
614	                yield from iter(x)
615	            elif x is not None:
616	                yield x
617	
618	    @property
619	    def is_terminator(self) -> bool:
620	        return self._is_terminator
621	
622	    @property
623	    def result_var(self) -> Var:
624	        if len(self.result_vars) != 1:
625	            raise ValueError(f"Operation {self.op} has {len(self.result_vars)} results")
626	        return self.result_vars[0]
627	
628	    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: "BytecodeContext"):
629	        raise NotImplementedError(f"Operation {self.op} must implement generate_bytecode")
630	
631	    def _to_string_block_prefixes(self) -> List[str]:
632	        return []
633	
634	    def _to_string_rhs(self) -> str:
635	        operands_str_list = []
636	        for name, val in self.operands.items():
637	            if isinstance(val, Var):
638	                operands_str_list.append(f"{name}={var_aggregate_name(val)}")
639	            elif isinstance(val, tuple) and all(isinstance(v, Var) for v in val):
640	                tup_str = ', '.join(var_aggregate_name(v) for v in val)
641	                operands_str_list.append(f"{name}=({tup_str})")
642	            elif val is None:
643	                operands_str_list.append(f"{name}=None")
644	            else:
645	                raise ValueError(f"Unexpected operand type: {type(val)}")
646	        operands_str = ", ".join(operands_str_list)
647	        if self.attributes:
648	            attr_parts = []
649	            for attr_name, attribute in self.attributes.items():
650	                if isinstance(attribute, str):
651	                    attr_parts.append(f'{attr_name}="{attribute}"')
652	                else:
653	                    attr_parts.append(f'{attr_name}={attribute}')
654	            attr_str = ", ".join(attr_parts)
655	        else:
656	            attr_str = ""
657	        delimiter_str = ", " if self.operands and self.attributes else ""
658	        return f"{self.op}({operands_str}{delimiter_str}{attr_str})"
659	
660	    def to_string(self,
661	                  indent: int = 0,
662	                  highlight_loc: Optional[Loc] = None,
663	                  include_loc: bool = False) -> str:
664	        indent_str = " " * indent
665	        lhs = (
666	            ", ".join(format_var(var) for var in self.result_vars)
667	            if self.result_vars
668	            else ""
669	        )
670	        rhs = self._to_string_rhs()
671	        loc_str = f"  // {self.loc}" if include_loc and self.loc else ""
672	
673	        result = f"{indent_str}{lhs + ' = ' if lhs else ''}{rhs}{loc_str}"
674	
675	        block_prefixs = self._to_string_block_prefixes()
676	        if len(block_prefixs) != len(self.nested_blocks):
677	            raise ValueError(
678	                f"Operation {self.op} has {len(block_prefixs)} block prefixes, "
679	                f"but {len(self.nested_blocks)} nested blocks"
680	            )
681	        for block_prefix, nested_block in zip(block_prefixs, self.nested_blocks):
682	            params_str = (" (" + ", ".join(format_var(v) for v in nested_block.params) + ")"
683	                          if len(nested_block.params) > 0 else "")
684	            result += f"\n{indent_str}{block_prefix}{params_str}\n" if block_prefix else "\n"
685	            block_str = nested_block.to_string(
686	                indent + 4,
687	                include_loc=include_loc
688	            )
689	            result += f"{block_str}"
690	
691	        if highlight_loc is not None and self.loc == highlight_loc:
692	            return f"\033[91m{result}\033[0m"
693	        return result
694	
695	    def __str__(self) -> str:
696	        return self.to_string()
697	
698	
699	def var_aggregate_name(var: Var) -> str:
700	    ret = var.name
701	    if var.is_aggregate():
702	        ret += "{" + ", ".join(x.name for x in var.flatten_aggregate()) + "}"
703	    return ret
704	
705	
706	def format_var(var: Var) -> str:
707	    ret = var_aggregate_name(var)
708	
709	    ty = var.try_get_type()
710	    if ty is not None:
711	        const_prefix = "const " if var.is_constant() else ""
712	        ret += f": {const_prefix}{ty}"
713	
714	    return ret
715	
716	
717	class Block:
718	    def __init__(self, ctx: IRContext, loc: Loc):
719	        self.ctx = ctx
720	        self._operations: List[Operation] = []
721	        self.params: tuple[Var, ...] = ()
722	        self.loc = loc
723	
724	    def empty_like_self(self: "Block") -> "Block":
725	        return Block(self.ctx, self.loc)
726	
727	    def append(self, op: Operation):
728	        self._operations.append(op)
729	
730	    def extend(self, ops: Sequence[Operation]):
731	        self._operations.extend(ops)
732	
733	    def __len__(self):
734	        return len(self._operations)
735	
736	    def __iter__(self):
737	        return iter(self._operations)
738	
739	    def __getitem__(self, i):
740	        return self._operations[i]
741	
742	    def __setitem__(self, i, value):
743	        if isinstance(i, slice):
744	            self._replace(i, value)
745	        else:
746	            self._replace(slice(i, i + 1), (value,))
747	
748	    def __delitem__(self, i):
749	        self._replace(i if isinstance(i, slice) else slice(i, i + 1), ())
750	
751	    def _replace(self, s: slice, new_ops: Sequence[Operation]):
752	        self._operations[s] = new_ops
753	
754	    def detach_all(self):
755	        ret, self._operations = self._operations, []
756	        return ret
757	
758	    @property
759	    def operations(self) -> Sequence[Operation]:
760	        return tuple(self._operations)
761	
762	    @operations.setter
763	    def operations(self, ops: Sequence[Operation]):
764	        self._operations = list(ops)
765	
766	    def make_temp_var(self, loc: Loc) -> Var:
767	        return self.ctx.make_temp(loc)
768	
769	    def make_temp_vars(self, loc: Loc, count: int) -> Tuple[Var, ...]:
770	        return tuple(self.ctx.make_temp(loc) for _ in range(count))
771	
772	    def to_string(self,
773	                  indent: int = 0,
774	                  highlight_loc: Optional[Loc] = None,
775	                  include_loc: bool = False) -> str:
776	        params = ", ".join(format_var(p) for p in self.params)
777	        ops = "\n".join(
778	            op.to_string(
779	                indent,
780	                highlight_loc,
781	                include_loc
782	            ) for op in self.operations
783	        )
784	        return f"{' ' * indent}({params}):\n{ops}"
785	
786	    def traverse(self) -> Iterator[Operation]:
787	        for op in self.operations:
788	            for b in op.nested_blocks:
789	                yield from b.traverse()
790	            yield op
791	
792	    def remove_if(self, predicate: Callable[[Operation], bool]):
793	        to_remove = {i for i, op in enumerate(self) if predicate(op)}
794	        remove_count = len(to_remove)
795	        if remove_count > 0:
796	            new_ops = [self[i] for i in range(len(self)) if i not in to_remove]
797	            self[:] = new_ops
798	
799	        for op in self:
800	            for nb in op.nested_blocks:
801	                remove_count += nb.remove_if(predicate)
802	
803	        return remove_count
804	
805	    def __str__(self) -> str:
806	        return self.to_string()
807

369	class FormattedPiece:
370	    """A single typed placeholder in a formatted string."""
371	    value_idx: int          # index into FormattedStringTy.value_types
372	    format_spec: str | None  # None = type-inferred; otherwise Python format spec (e.g. '.2f')

376	class StringFormat:
377	    """Immutable format template for a formatted string value."""
378	    pieces: tuple[str | FormattedPiece, ...]

381	@dataclass(frozen=True)
382	class FormattedStringTy(Type):
383	    format: "StringFormat"
384	    value_types: tuple
385	    has_single_quote: bool = False
386	    has_double_quote: bool = False
387	
388	    def is_aggregate(self) -> bool:
389	        return True
390	
391	    def aggregate_item_types(self) -> tuple:
392	        return self.value_types
393	
394	    def make_aggregate_value(self, items: tuple) -> "AggregateValue":
395	        return FormattedStringValue(self.format, items)
396	
397	    def __str__(self):
398	        parts = []
399	        for piece in self.format.pieces:
400	            if isinstance(piece, str):
401	                parts.append(piece)
402	            else:
403	                ty = self.value_types[piece.value_idx]
404	                if piece.format_spec is not None:
405	                    parts.append(f"{{<{ty}>:{piece.format_spec}}}")
406	                else:
407	                    parts.append(f"{{<{ty}>}}")
408	        return 'FormattedString<"' + "".join(parts) + '">'

427	class TileTy(TensorLikeTy):
428	    def __new__(cls, dtype: "DType", shape: Sequence[int] = ()) -> "TileTy":
429	        shape = tuple(shape)
430	        try:
431	            return _tile_ty_cache[(dtype, shape)]
432	        except KeyError:
433	            pass
434	
435	        assert isinstance(dtype, DType)
436	        ret = object.__new__(cls)
437	        ret.dtype = dtype
438	        ret.shape = shape
439	        _tile_ty_cache[(dtype, shape)] = ret
440	        return ret
441	
442	    @override
443	    def tensor_dtype(self) -> "DType":
444	        return self.dtype
445	
446	    @override
447	    def tensor_shape(self) -> tuple[int, ...]:
448	        return self.shape
449	
450	    @override
451	    def make_symbol(self, var: "Var") -> Symbol:
452	        return SymbolicTile(var)
453	
454	    @property
455	    def ndim(self):
456	        return len(self.shape)
457	
458	    @property
459	    def numel(self):
460	        return reduce(operator.mul, self.shape, 1)
461	
462	    def __eq__(self, other: Type):
463	        if isinstance(other, TileTy):
464	            return self.dtype == other.dtype and self.shape == other.shape
465	        return False
466	
467	    def __hash__(self):
468	        return hash(("TileTy", self.dtype, self.shape))
469	
470	    def __repr__(self):
471	        return f"TileTy(dtype={self.dtype}, shape={self.shape})"
472	
473	    def __str__(self):
474	        shape_str = "(" + ','.join(str(x) for x in self.shape) + ")"
475	        return f"Tile[{self.dtype},{shape_str}]"
476	
477	
478	_tile_ty_cache: dict[tuple["DType", tuple[int, ...]], TileTy] = dict()

481	class SymbolicTile(Symbol, Tile):
482	    def __init__(self, var: "Var"):
483	        Symbol.__init__(self, var)

485	    @property
486	    def dtype(self):
487	        ty = self._var.get_type()
488	        assert isinstance(ty, TileTy)
489	        return ty.dtype
490	
491	    @property
492	    def shape(self) -> tuple[int, ...]:
493	        ty = self._var.get_type()
494	        assert isinstance(ty, TileTy)
495	        return ty.shape
496	
497	    @property
498	    def ndim(self) -> int:
499	        return len(self.shape)
500	
501	    def __bool__(self):
502	        raise TileValueError("Symbolic tile has no concrete value and thus cannot be converted"
503	                             " to boolean")
504	
505	    def __int__(self):
506	        raise TileValueError("Symbolic tile has no concrete value and thus cannot be converted"
507	                             " to an integer")
508	
509	    def __float__(self):
510	        raise TileValueError("Symbolic tile has no concrete value and thus cannot be converted"
511	                             " to a float")
512	
513	    def __index__(self):
514	        raise TileValueError("Symbolic tile has no concrete value and thus cannot be converted"
515	                             " to an integer")

518	        return f"<tile[{self.dtype}, {self.shape}]>"

521	# ============== Array Type ===============
522	
523	
524	class ArrayTy(Type):
525	    def __init__(self,
526	                 dtype: "DType",
527	                 /,
528	                 shape: Tuple[Optional[int], ...],
529	                 strides: Tuple[Optional[int], ...],
530	                 typing_hooks: "TypingHooks",
531	                 index_dtype=None,
532	                 memory_space: MemorySpace = MemorySpace.GENERIC):
533	        from .._datatype import int32, DType
534	        assert isinstance(dtype, DType)
535	        self.dtype = dtype
536	        self.shape = shape
537	        self.strides = strides
538	        self.index_dtype = int32 if index_dtype is None else index_dtype
539	        self.memory_space = memory_space
540	        self.typing_hooks = typing_hooks
541	
542	    def make_symbol(self, var: "Var") -> Any:
543	        return SymbolicArray(var)
544	
545	    def is_aggregate(self) -> bool:
546	        # Even though arrays are actually represented with TensorViews, they can't be
547	        # propagated through control flow. So we need to be able to unpack the array
548	        # into its individual (base_ptr, *shape, *strides) values.
549	        return True
550	
551	    def aggregate_item_types(self) -> tuple["Type", ...]:
552	        from .._datatype import pointer_dtype
553	        base_ptr_ty = pointer_dtype(self.dtype, self.memory_space)
554	        base_ptr_tile_ty = self.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(base_ptr_ty, ())
555	        size_ty = self.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(self.index_dtype, ())
556	        return (base_ptr_tile_ty,) + (size_ty,) * (self.ndim * 2)
557	
558	    def make_aggregate_value(self, items: tuple["Var", ...]) -> "AggregateValue":
559	        assert len(items) == 1 + 2 * self.ndim
560	        return ArrayValue(items[0], items[1:self.ndim + 1], items[self.ndim + 1:])
561	
562	    @property
563	    def ndim(self):
564	        return len(self.shape)
565	
566	    def __eq__(self, other: Type):
567	        return (isinstance(other, ArrayTy)
568	                and self.dtype == other.dtype
569	                and self.shape == other.shape
570	                and self.strides == other.strides
571	                and self.index_dtype == other.index_dtype
572	                and self.memory_space == other.memory_space)
573	
574	    def __hash__(self):
575	        return hash(("ArrayTy", self.dtype, self.shape, self.strides, self.index_dtype,
576	                     self.memory_space))
577	
578	    def __str__(self):
579	        from .._datatype import int32
580	        shape_str = ('?' if x is None else str(x) for x in self.shape)
581	        shape_str = "(" + ','.join(shape_str) + ")"
582	        strides_str = ('?' if x is None else str(x) for x in self.strides)
583	        strides_str = "(" + ','.join(strides_str) + ")"
584	        indexty_str = "" if self.index_dtype == int32 else f",index_dtype={self.index_dtype}]"
585	        memspc = "" if self.memory_space == MemorySpace.GENERIC else f", {self.memory_space}"
586	        return f"Array[{self.dtype},{shape_str}:{strides_str}{indexty_str}{memspc}]"
587	
588	
589	@dataclass
590	class ArrayValue(AggregateValue):
591	    base_ptr: "Var"
592	    shape: tuple["Var", ...]
593	    strides: tuple["Var", ...]
594	
595	    def as_tuple(self) -> tuple["Var", ...]:
596	        return self.base_ptr, *self.shape, *self.strides
597

599	class SymbolicArray(Symbol, Array):

601	        Symbol.__init__(self, var)

603	    @property
604	    def dtype(self):
605	        ty = self._var.get_type()
606	        assert isinstance(ty, ArrayTy)
607	        return ty.dtype

609	    @property
610	    def shape(self):
611	        agg = self._var.get_aggregate()
612	        assert isinstance(agg, ArrayValue)
613	        return tuple(var2sym(v) for v in agg.shape)
614	
615	    @property
616	    def strides(self):
617	        agg = self._var.get_aggregate()
618	        assert isinstance(agg, ArrayValue)
619	        return tuple(var2sym(v) for v in agg.strides)
620	
621	    @property
622	    def ndim(self) -> int:
623	        ty = self._var.get_type()
624	        assert isinstance(ty, ArrayTy)
625	        return ty.ndim
626	
627	    def __repr__(self):
628	        ty = self._var.get_type()
629	        assert isinstance(ty, ArrayTy)
630	
631	        shape_str = ", ".join("?" if s is None else str(s) for s in ty.shape)
632	
633	        return f"<array[{ty.dtype}, ({shape_str})]>"

968	# Placeholder object for use as an inspect.Parameter's default value inside
969	# signatures of closures.
970	@dataclass(frozen=True)
971	class ClosureDefaultPlaceholder:
972	    # Index into `ClosureTy.default_value_types` and `ClosureValue.default_values`.
973	    default_value_index: int
974	
975	
976	@dataclass(frozen=True)
977	class LiveCapturedScope:
978	    depth: int
979	    local_scope: "LocalScope"
980	
981	
982	@dataclass(frozen=True)
983	class ClosureTy(Type):
984	    func_hir: "hir.Function"
985	    default_value_types: tuple[Type, ...]
986	
987	    # Lists all enclosing functions' scopes that are still live.
988	    captured_scopes: tuple[LiveCapturedScope, ...]
989	
990	    frozen_capture_types_by_depth: tuple[tuple[Type, ...] | None]
991	
992	    def make_symbol(self, var: "Var") -> Symbol:
993	        return SymbolicClosure(var)
994	
995	    def is_aggregate(self) -> bool:
996	        return True
997	
998	    def aggregate_item_types(self) -> tuple["Type", ...]:
999	        return (
1000	            *self.default_value_types,
1001	            *(t for types in self.frozen_capture_types_by_depth
1002	              if types is not None for t in types)
1003	        )
1004	
1005	    def make_aggregate_value(self, items: tuple["Var", ...]) -> "AggregateValue":
1006	        it = iter(items)
1007	        default_values = tuple(next(it) for _ in self.default_value_types)
1008	        frozen_captures_by_depth = tuple(None if types is None else tuple(next(it) for _ in types)
1009	                                         for types in self.frozen_capture_types_by_depth)
1010	        assert next(it, None) is None
1011	        return ClosureValue(default_values=default_values,
1012	                            frozen_captures_by_depth=frozen_captures_by_depth)
1013	
1014	    def __str__(self):
1015	        ret = f"Closure[{self.func_hir.desc.short_str()}"
1016	        if len(self.default_value_types) > 0:
1017	            default_strings = []
1018	            for p in self.func_hir.signature.parameters.values():
1019	                if p.default is not inspect.Parameter.empty:
1020	                    assert isinstance(p.default, ClosureDefaultPlaceholder)
1021	                    default_ty = self.default_value_types[p.default.default_value_index]
1022	                    default_strings.append(f"'{p.name}': {default_ty}")
1023	            ret += ", defaults={" + ", ".join(default_strings) + "}"
1024	        if any(x is not None and len(x) > 0 for x in self.frozen_capture_types_by_depth):
1025	            capture_strings = []
1026	            for types, local_indices, parent_func in zip(self.frozen_capture_types_by_depth,
1027	                                                         self.func_hir.captures_by_depth,
1028	                                                         self.func_hir.enclosing_funcs,
1029	                                                         strict=True):
1030	                if types is None:
1031	                    continue
1032	                for ty, idx in zip(types, local_indices, strict=True):
1033	                    name = parent_func.local_names[idx]
1034	                    capture_strings.append(f"'{name}': {ty}")
1035	            ret += ", frozen_captures={" + ", ".join(capture_strings) + "}"
1036	
1037	        return ret + "]"
1038	
1039	
1040	@dataclass
1041	class ClosureValue(AggregateValue):
1042	    # Default values of parameters. These need to be carried by the closure's value
1043	    # because default expressions are evaluated at definition time, not when the closure is called.
1044	    # Should have the same length as the corresponding `ClosureTy.default_value_types`.
1045	    default_values: tuple["Var", ...]
1046	
1047	    # Tuple of the same length as `ty.func_hir.enclosing_functions`
1048	    # and `ty.frozen_capture_types_by_depth`, where `ty` is the `ClosureTy` of this closure.
1049	    #
1050	    # For each depth `i`, `frozen_captures_by_depth[i]` is either:
1051	    #   - None: means the enclosing function's LocalScope is still live;
1052	    #   - tuple[Var, ...]: means the enclosing function's LocalScope is no longer live.
1053	    #       The tuple contains the final values of the variables captured from the enclosing
1054	    #       function's scope. Its length should be the same as `ty.func_hir.captures_by_depth`.
1055	    frozen_captures_by_depth: tuple[tuple["Var", ...] | None, ...]
1056	
1057	    def as_tuple(self) -> tuple["Var", ...]:
1058	        return (
1059	            *self.default_values,
1060	            *(v for values in self.frozen_captures_by_depth
1061	              if values is not None for v in values)
1062	        )
1063	
1064	
1065	class SymbolicClosure(Symbol):
1066	    def __repr__(self):
1067	        ty = self._var.get_type()
1068	        assert isinstance(ty, ClosureTy)
1069	        desc = ty.func_hir.desc
1070	        what = "lambda" if desc.name is None else f"function '{desc.name}'"
1071	        filename = os.path.basename(desc.filename)
1072	        return f"<{what} @{filename}:{desc.line}>"
1073	
1074	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
1075	        from cuda.tile._dispatch_mode import DispatchMode
1076	        return DispatchMode().get_current().call_tile_function_from_host(self, args, kwargs)
1077

29	def _verify_params_match(stub_sig: inspect.Signature, func_sig: inspect.Signature):
30	    assert len(stub_sig.parameters) == len(func_sig.parameters), (
31	        f"Stub and implementation must have same number of parameters."
32	        f" Signatures: {stub_sig}, {func_sig}.")
33	    for i, (stub_param, func_param) in enumerate(zip(stub_sig.parameters.values(),
34	                                                     func_sig.parameters.values(), strict=True)):
35	        assert stub_param.name == func_param.name, (
36	            f"Stub and implementation have different parameter names at position {i}"
37	            f" Signatures: {stub_sig}, {func_sig}.")

40	@dataclass(frozen=True)
41	class WildcardClass:
42	    pass
43	
44	
45	# Can be used as a parameter of an overloaded implementation to match any value.
46	#
47	# For example, a getattr() implementation for a specific type may choose to handle
48	# all attributes in a generic way, instead of registering an individual overload
49	# for each attribute. In this case, one can use WILDCARD in place of the attribute name.
50	WILDCARD = WildcardClass()

53	class OverloadNotFoundError(Exception):
54	    def __init__(self, found_overload_matching_first_param: bool):
55	        self.found_overload_matching_first_param = found_overload_matching_first_param
56

58	class ImplRegistry:
59	    def __init__(self):
60	        self.op_implementations = dict()
61	        self._overloaded_implementations = defaultdict(dict)
62	        self.unflatten_aggregate_implementations = dict()
63	
64	    @staticmethod
65	    def get_current() -> "ImplRegistry":
66	        ret = _current_registry.impl_registry
67	        assert ret is not None
68	        return ret
69	
70	    @contextmanager
71	    def as_current(self):
72	        old = _current_registry.impl_registry
73	        _current_registry.impl_registry = self
74	        try:
75	            yield self
76	        finally:
77	            _current_registry.impl_registry = old
78	
79	    def update(self, source: "ImplRegistry"):
80	        self.op_implementations.update(source.op_implementations)
81	        for stub, overloads in source._overloaded_implementations.items():
82	            self._overloaded_implementations[stub].update(overloads)
83	        self.unflatten_aggregate_implementations.update(source.unflatten_aggregate_implementations)
84	
85	    def overload_dispatcher(self, stub, *, fixed_args: Sequence[Any] = ()):
86	        """
87	        Decorates a function to attach an overloaded implementation dispatcher to a stub.
88	
89	        The decorated function must yield an "overload key", i.e. a tuple of values based
90	        on which the appropriate overload should be selected, and then handle
91	        the OverloadNotFoundError by re-raising a TileError.
92	        See getattr_overload_dispatcher() for an example.
93	        """
94	
95	        def decorate(key_func):
96	            orig_func = key_func
97	            if len(fixed_args) > 0:
98	                key_func = functools.partial(orig_func, *fixed_args)
99	
100	            @functools.wraps(key_func)
101	            async def implementation(*args):
102	                generator = key_func(*args)
103	                key = next(generator)
104	                cur_registry = ImplRegistry.get_current()
105	                overload_impl = cur_registry._find_overload(stub, key)
106	                if overload_impl is None:
107	                    generator.throw(OverloadNotFoundError(
108	                            cur_registry._have_overload_matching_first_param(stub, key[0])))
109	                    raise RuntimeError("Expected the overload key provider to re-throw a TileError")
110	
111	                try:
112	                    next(generator)
113	                except StopIteration:
114	                    pass  # Good, that's what we expect
115	                else:
116	                    raise RuntimeError("Expected the overload key provider to yield exactly once")
117	
118	                result = overload_impl(*args)
119	                if overload_impl._is_coroutine:
120	                    result = await result
121	
122	                return result
123	
124	            self.impl(stub)(implementation)
125	            return orig_func
126	
127	        return decorate
128	
129	    def _find_overload(self, stub: Callable, overload: tuple[Any, ...]) -> Callable | None:
130	        candidates = self._overloaded_implementations[stub]
131	        best_matches = []
132	        best_priority = -1
133	
134	        for priority, predicates, impl in candidates.values():
135	            if priority < best_priority:
136	                continue
137	
138	            if not all(p(arg) for p, arg in zip(predicates, overload, strict=True)):
139	                continue
140	
141	            if priority > best_priority:
142	                best_matches.clear()
143	                best_priority = priority
144	
145	            best_matches.append(impl)
146	
147	        match best_matches:
148	            case []:
149	                return None
150	            case [x]:
151	                return x
152	            case _:
153	                raise ValueError(f"Multiple matching overloads found for {stub}, {overload}")
154	
155	    def impl(self, stub, *, fixed_args: Sequence[Any] = (),
156	             min_version: Optional[BytecodeVersion] = None,
157	             overload: tuple[Any, ...] = ()):
158	        stub_sig = get_signature(stub)
159	
160	        def _check_version():
161	            cur_version = Builder.get_current().ir_ctx.tileiras_version
162	            if min_version is not None and cur_version < min_version:
163	                raise TileUnsupportedFeatureError(
164	                    f"{stub.__name__} requires tileiras "
165	                    f"{min_version.as_string()} or later. "
166	                    f"Current version is {cur_version.as_string()}."
167	                )
168	
169	        def decorate(func):
170	            orig_func = func
171	            if len(fixed_args) > 0:
172	                func = functools.partial(orig_func, *fixed_args)
173	
174	            func_sig = inspect.signature(func)
175	            _verify_params_match(stub_sig, func_sig)
176	            is_coroutine = inspect.iscoroutinefunction(func)
177	            if is_coroutine:
178	                @functools.wraps(func)
179	                async def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
180	                    _check_version()
181	                    # Memorize the stub and the args so that we can automatically
182	                    # provide context for error messages.
183	                    old = _current_stub.stub_and_args
184	                    _current_stub.stub_and_args = (stub, stub_sig, func_sig, args, kwargs)
185	                    try:
186	                        return await func(*args, **kwargs)
187	                    finally:
188	                        _current_stub.stub_and_args = old
189	            else:
190	                @functools.wraps(func)
191	                def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
192	                    _check_version()
193	                    # Memorize the stub and the args so that we can automatically
194	                    # provide context for error messages.
195	                    old = _current_stub.stub_and_args
196	                    _current_stub.stub_and_args = (stub, stub_sig, func_sig, args, kwargs)
197	                    try:
198	                        return func(*args, **kwargs)
199	                    finally:
200	                        _current_stub.stub_and_args = old
201	
202	            wrapper._is_coroutine = is_coroutine
203	
204	            if len(overload) == 0:
205	                self.op_implementations[stub] = wrapper
206	            else:
207	                predicates = tuple(_predicate_from_overload_pattern(p) for p in overload)
208	                self._overloaded_implementations[stub][overload] = \
209	                    (sum(p != WILDCARD for p in overload), predicates, wrapper)
210	
211	            return orig_func
212	
213	        return decorate
214	
215	    def _have_overload_matching_first_param(self, stub: Callable, first_param: Any) -> bool:
216	        candidates = self._overloaded_implementations[stub]
217	        return any(predicates[0](first_param)
218	                   for _priority, predicates, _impl in candidates.values())
219	
220	    def unflatten_aggregate_impl(self, aggregate_type_class: type[Type]):
221	        def decorate(func):
222	            self.unflatten_aggregate_implementations[aggregate_type_class] = func
223	            return func
224	        return decorate
225	
226	
227	def _predicate_from_overload_pattern(pattern):
228	    if pattern == WILDCARD:
229	        return lambda _: True
230	    elif isinstance(pattern, type):
231	        return lambda x: issubclass(x, pattern)
232	    else:
233	        return lambda x: pattern == x

256	def require_constant_int(var: Var) -> int:
257	    if not var.is_constant():
258	        raise _make_type_error("Expected an integer constant, but given value is not constant", var)
259	    ty = var.get_type()
260	    if not is_scalar(ty, is_integral):
261	        raise _make_type_error(f"Expected an integer constant, but given value has type {ty}", var)
262	    return var.get_constant()

280	def require_constant_scalar(var: Var) -> bool | int | float:
281	    ty = var.get_type()
282	    if not is_scalar(ty):
283	        raise _make_type_error(f"Expected a scalar constant, but given value has type {ty}", var)
284	    if not var.is_constant():
285	        raise _make_type_error(f"Expected a constant, but given value has non-constant type {ty}",
286	                               var)
287	    ret = var.get_constant()
288	    assert isinstance(ret, bool | int | float)
289	    return ret
